articles.headline: Rediff files for ~800 crore IPO confidentially
articles.summary: Rediff, a subsidiary of AvenuesAI, has confidentially filed its DRHP for an IPO aiming to raise ~600-800 crore. Founded in 1996 and first listed on Nasdaq in 2000, Rediff now offers enterprise email, cloud storage, e-commerce, and a financial services platform.

articles.headline: Strong order pipeline for listed shipbuilding majors
articles.summary: India's three listed shipyards (MDL, GRSEL, CSL) have robust order visibility due to Indian Navy's 21% share in defence spending and a large order pipeline of over ~3 trillion for the next decade. GRSEL delivered 8 vessels in FY26, and CSL has a ~23,000 crore order from CMA of France.

articles.headline: Powerica, Sai Parenterals, Amir Chand Jagdish Kumar debut today
articles.summary: Shares of Powerica, Sai Parenterals, and Amir Chand Jagdish Kumar will debut on stock exchanges today after their IPOs closed last week. The three IPOs saw modest demand, with subscription levels between 1.1x and 3.4x.

articles.headline: Bvishal Oil and Energy files for IPO
articles.summary: Onshore oilfield services provider Bvishal Oil and Energy filed preliminary papers with SEBI for an initial public offering (IPO). The proposed IPO comprises a fresh issue of 1.8 crore shares and an offer for sale (OFS) of 7.7 million shares.

articles.headline: Equities may outperform most asset classes in FY27
articles.summary: Despite the West Asia war, AI worries, and trade fears, analysts expect Indian equities to outperform most asset classes in FY27, after Nifty and Sensex's worst FY26 performance in six years. A longer-term view suggests mid-to-high single-digit returns, with Nifty potentially reaching 24,000-26,000.

articles.headline: Corporate travel sector eyes growth amid global flux
articles.summary: Corporate travel is a growing focus area for travel majors, with India's business travel spending estimated at $38.3 billion in 2024. Despite global flux, firms seek efficiency and cost optimization, driving adoption of technology-enabled travel management. The market size is projected to reach $81.54 billion by 2034.

articles.headline: Blinkit launches services inside Mumbai airport
articles.summary: Quick commerce platform Blinkit launched services inside Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport (Terminal 2, domestic departures) in Mumbai. Air travellers can order from 2,500+ products, delivered within the terminal by a dedicated team of walkers, marking a first for airports globally.

articles.headline: Equities open FY27 on a high amid hopes of war ending
articles.summary: Indian equities started the new fiscal year positively, driven by global cues and hopes that the West Asia war will end soon. The Nifty 50 settled up 1.56% at 22,679.40, and the BSE Sensex ended 1.65% higher at 73,134.32. Defence, capital markets, PSU banks, and chemicals sectors saw significant gains.

articles.headline: Govt limits domestic ATF price hike to 25%; IndiGo ups surcharge
articles.summary: State oil marketing companies have limited the aviation turbine fuel (ATF) rate hike to 25% for scheduled domestic flights to soften the blow for airlines. IndiGo hiked its fuel surcharge on domestic and international flights. Foreign routes will pay for the full increase in ATF prices.

articles.headline: PV makers end FY26 on positive note
articles.summary: All major passenger vehicle makers, including Maruti Suzuki India, Mahindra & Mahindra, Tata Motors, Hyundai Motor India, and Toyota Kirloskar Motor, reported growth in their domestic wholesales in March 2026. Total dispatches to dealers reached around 4.48 lakh units, up from 3.86 lakh in March last year. Two-wheeler sales also grew.

articles.headline: Raghunathan appointed CFO of MM Forgings
articles.summary: MM Forgings has appointed R Raghunathan as the company’s Chief Financial Officer (CFO) and Key Managerial Personnel, effective April 1, 2026. He succeeds R Venkatakrishnan, who attained superannuation in May 2022 but continued as CFO for nearly four years post-retirement.

articles.headline: E3W players eye 7–10% price hikes outpacing ICE
articles.summary: Electric three-wheeler manufacturers are contemplating price hikes of 7–10% in early FY27, notably higher than the 0.5–1.5% increases by ICE vehicle makers. This is driven by rising costs of battery components, logistics, a weaker rupee, and geopolitical disruptions affecting imports of critical inputs.

articles.headline: Ashok Leyland to raise LCVs’ addressable market
articles.summary: Ashok Leyland plans to expand its addressable market in the light commercial vehicle (LCV) segment from 54% to around 80% over the next three to four years. This involves entering new categories like sub-2 tonne and 4–6 tonne segments, as well as small passenger vehicles, with two new product launches planned.

articles.headline: Cabot Sanmar to invest ₹220 cr in fumed silica plant
articles.summary: Cabot Sanmar, a joint venture of US’ Cabot Corporation and The Sanmar Group, is investing ₹220 crore for brownfield expansion of its fumed silica manufacturing facility at Mettur, near Salem. The plan is to double capacity to meet growing domestic demand, with commissioning expected in Q4 2027.

articles.headline: Citi top executive flags lasting war cost
articles.summary: Deirdre Dunn, Citigroup's rates-trading chief, is closely monitoring fertilizer and aluminum markets to assess the knock-on effects of the Iran war on inflation and central bank reactions. She notes that traders are settling in for the long haul, anticipating significant and lasting implications of the energy shock on the real economy, urging vigilance on who bears the costs.

articles.headline: NSDL waives settlement fees for women opening new a/cs
articles.summary: The National Securities Depository (NSDL) has launched a 'Women Plan' to encourage greater participation of women in capital markets. Under this initiative, women opening a demat account between April 1, 2026, and March 31, 2027, will receive a complete waiver of settlement fees on debit instructions for three years.

articles.headline: RMC Switchgears moves to mainboard, hits ceiling
articles.summary: Shares of RMC Switchgears surged 5% to close at the upper circuit of ₹370.90 on Wednesday, buoyed by its migration from the SME platform to the mainboard of BSE and a fresh listing on the NSE. This reflected strong investor demand following the milestone development.

articles.headline: With Patni Family Office hiking stake, Ugro Capital jumps 9%
articles.summary: Shares of Ugro Capital jumped 9% after the Patni Family Office crossed a 5% ownership stake, indicating a conviction-driven investment. This follows backing from institutional investors like IFU and Samena Capital, who support Ugro Capital's green financing initiatives for MSMEs.

articles.headline: Rain Industries gains 8 per cent as unit completes EV battery graphite project
articles.summary: Rain Industries shares closed 7.88% higher after its wholly-owned subsidiary, Rain Carbon Canada Inc, announced the successful completion of a joint project. The initiative produced coated spherical purified graphite, a key material for electric vehicle (EV) battery anodes, which offers lower operating costs and a reduced carbon footprint.

articles.headline: Crisil reaffirms rating on Thomas Cook, stock rises
articles.summary: Thomas Cook (India) received a reaffirmation of its Crisil AA/Stable and Crisil A1+ credit ratings, the highest for an Indian travel and tourism company. Shares rose 6.62%. Crisil cited strong backing from Canadian parent Fairfax Financial Holdings and Thomas Cook India’s market leadership.

articles.headline: Two dozen cos filed draft IPO papers in last two days of FY26
articles.summary: More than two dozen companies filed preliminary papers with SEBI to raise funds through initial public offerings (IPOs) in the last two days of FY26. These firms, including Sadbhav Futuretech, Punjab Carbonic, and Adroit Industries, aim to utilize the proceeds for working capital, debt repayment, and expansion plans.

articles.headline: NMDC hits highest-ever output of 53 mt in FY26
articles.summary: The National Mineral Development Corporation (NMDC) achieved a landmark with production of 53 million tonnes (mt) of iron ore in FY26, becoming the first company in India’s mining history to surpass the 50 mt annual production mark. This represents a 51% year-on-year growth.

articles.headline: Coal India FY26 output falls 1.7%, misses annual target
articles.summary: Coal India’s total production for FY26 witnessed a 1.7% year-on-year fall at 768.1 million tonnes (mt), missing its annual target of 875 mt. Four of its seven subsidiaries saw production declines. Coal offtake also decreased by 2.4%, while e-auction prices rose 45% in March.

articles.headline: Palm oil falls despite Indonesia’s biodiesel push
articles.summary: Malaysian palm oil fell on Wednesday, reversing four straight sessions of gains, despite Indonesia’s plans to proceed with the rollout of its B50 biodiesel blending plan. The benchmark palm oil contract for June delivery on the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Exchange shed 60 ringgit to 4,768 ringgit a tonne at closing.

articles.headline: Copper up on hopes of quick end to Iran war
articles.summary: Copper prices extended gains on Wednesday, hitting their highest in two weeks, on hopes that the Iran war is close to ending. Benchmark three-month copper on the LME was up 0.2% at $12,364 a tonne by 1400 GMT, after earlier touching $12,492.50.

articles.headline: Gold rallies 3% on global cues on Iran’s soft signal
articles.summary: Gold prices increased by ₹4,120, or 3%, on Wednesday to ₹1,50,853 per 10 g, on the back of firm global trends. This rally was fueled by global cues, including signals from Iran suggesting a potential end to the conflict, and bargain hunting by investors.

articles.headline: Punjab exporters propose basmati-for-oil barter with Iran
articles.summary: The Punjab Rice Millers Exporters Association has urged the Centre to explore a “barter agreement” with Iran. They proposed importing crude oil from Tehran in exchange for premium aromatic basmati rice, with payments settled in Indian rupees, to ease India's oil crisis and revive trade ties disrupted by the war.

articles.headline: BASF expands in India with Noble Seeds buy
articles.summary: BASF’s vegetable seeds business has completed the acquisition of Noble Seeds Pvt Ltd, strengthening its long-term growth ambitions in India’s dynamic vegetable seed market. Noble Seeds adds strong genetics in cauliflower and radish, complementing BASF | Nunhems' existing strengths and enabling a more complete offer for Indian growers.

articles.headline: Govt introduces voluntary ‘Tea Mark’ quality certification scheme
articles.summary: The State-run Tea Board is introducing the ‘Tea Mark’ quality certification scheme in May to promote quality consumption and marketing of teas. This voluntary scheme ensures that certified tea meets prescribed quality parameters and food safety standards, aiming to curb issues like adulteration and misrepresentation of origin through digital traceability.

articles.headline: Kolkata firm offers cars, tractors to farmers to source maize
articles.summary: Regaal Resources Ltd, a Kolkata-based agro-processing firm, offers incentives like cars, tractors, motorcycles, and cash to farmers in Bihar and West Bengal who supply large quantities of corn. This unique procurement strategy helps the company secure maize for its wet processing mill and rewards growers for their loyalty.

articles.headline: At $18 b, PE-VC exits down 40% in FY26
articles.summary: Private Equity-Venture Capital (PE-VC) exits in FY26 declined 40% year-on-year to $18.8 billion across 223 deals, reflecting volatile market conditions and a more strategic approach by Indian investors. Total PE-VC funding also saw a 10% drop to $33.9 billion, with mega deals and healthcare funding notably impacted.

articles.headline: CBI files one more case against RCom, Anil Ambani
articles.summary: The CBI has filed another case against Reliance Communications (RCom) Ltd and its erstwhile promoter Anil Ambani, alleging cheating and misappropriating ₹3,750 crore from the Life Insurance Corporation (LIC) of India. The complaint, based on a forensic audit report, alleges fraudulent inducement for NCD subscriptions and misuse of funds.

articles.headline: RoSCTL Scheme for textile exports extended till September 30
articles.summary: The Textiles Ministry has extended the Rebate of State and Central Taxes and Levies (RoSCTL) Scheme for exports of apparel/garments and made-ups by six months, until September 30. The RoDTEP Scheme for other textile products was also similarly extended. This aims to strengthen export competitiveness, particularly for MSMEs.

articles.headline: Adani Green adds over 5 GW capacity in FY26, most of it at Khavda RE park
articles.summary: Adani Green Energy Ltd (AGEL) added over 5 GW of renewable energy capacity in FY26, primarily driven by its Khavda mega project in Gujarat. This expansion, one of the largest globally, boosts its total operational portfolio to 19.3 GW and includes commissioning 1,376 MWh of battery energy storage systems (BESS) at Khavda.

articles.headline: Jazeera Airways adds India-Kuwait route
articles.summary: Jazeera Airways is expanding its India-Kuwait air bridge by operating additional flights to Kannur, Kozhikode, Mangaluru, and Tiruchirapalli. These services will be operated from Dammam in Saudi Arabia, facilitating passenger and cargo movement, especially after Kuwait International Airport's closure due to the Iran conflict.

articles.headline: IndiGo to operate 2,000 daily domestic flights this summer
articles.summary: Airline major IndiGo is set to operate up to 2,000 daily domestic flights this summer, recovering from a 10% capacity cap imposed in December. The airline has undertaken multiple operational measures, strengthened staffing, and is expanding its network with new international routes like Shanghai and Reunion Island, along with increased domestic regional services.

articles.headline: Business Leaders Discuss Market Trends
articles.summary: Leading business figures convened to discuss current market trends and future investment landscapes. The dialogue focused on fostering innovation and addressing challenges faced by small and medium enterprises.

articles.headline: Startup Ecosystem Sees Growth
articles.summary: The national startup ecosystem has witnessed considerable growth, attracting new investments and fostering innovation. Government support programs are credited for this positive trend in entrepreneurship.

articles.headline: Business Sector Investments Increase
articles.summary: The business sector has seen a significant increase in domestic and foreign investments, signaling strong confidence in the economic climate. New ventures are expected to create employment opportunities.

articles.headline: Corporate Social Responsibility Trends
articles.summary: Corporate social responsibility trends are shifting towards more impactful and sustainable initiatives. Businesses are increasingly integrating social and environmental goals into their core strategies.

articles.headline: Manufacturing Sector Growth Reported
articles.summary: The manufacturing sector reported significant growth in the last quarter, driven by increased demand and new production capacities. This positive trend is contributing to overall economic recovery.

articles.headline: Small Business Support Schemes
articles.summary: New support schemes for small businesses were introduced to foster entrepreneurship and economic resilience. These initiatives provide financial assistance and mentorship to emerging enterprises.

articles.headline: Foreign Direct Investment Inflow
articles.summary: The country witnessed a significant inflow of foreign direct investment, indicating investor confidence in the economy. The new investments are expected to create jobs and boost industrial growth.

articles.headline: Retail Sector Adapts to New Trends
articles.summary: The retail sector is adapting to new consumer trends and technological advancements. Online sales continue to grow, prompting traditional retailers to enhance their digital presence and offerings.

articles.headline: Automotive Sector Trends Analysis
articles.summary: An analysis of automotive sector trends indicates a shift towards electric vehicles and sustainable manufacturing. Companies are investing in R&D to meet future market demands and environmental regulations.

articles.headline: Textile Industry Innovation
articles.summary: Innovation in the textile industry is driving new product development and sustainable manufacturing practices. Companies are adopting eco-friendly materials and advanced production technologies to stay competitive.

articles.headline: Agricultural Export Growth
articles.summary: Agricultural exports saw significant growth, contributing positively to the national trade balance. New market access and quality standards are driving this success for farmers and producers.

articles.headline: Financial Sector Regulatory Updates
articles.summary: Updates to financial sector regulations aim to enhance stability and protect consumers. These changes address issues like market manipulation, digital transactions, and financial product transparency.

articles.headline: Export Promotion Council Meeting
articles.summary: The Export Promotion Council held a meeting to discuss strategies for increasing national exports. Focus areas include market diversification, trade agreements, and support for export-oriented businesses.

articles.headline: Manufacturing Output Increases
articles.summary: Manufacturing output showed a significant increase, indicating a robust industrial recovery. Policy support and rising consumer demand are driving growth in key manufacturing sectors.

articles.headline: FMCG Sector Records Strong Sales
articles.summary: The Fast-Moving Consumer Goods (FMCG) sector recorded strong sales growth, driven by increased consumer spending and product innovation. Companies are adapting to changing market demands and preferences.

articles.headline: Tourism Sector Recovery
articles.summary: The tourism sector is showing signs of strong recovery, driven by domestic travel and international visitor arrivals. Government support and promotional campaigns are boosting the industry's resurgence.

articles.headline: Stock Market Performance Review
articles.summary: The stock market concluded the week with mixed results, reflecting investor caution amid global economic uncertainties. Analysts provided a detailed review of sectoral performance and future outlook.

articles.headline: Digital Payments Adoption Surges
articles.summary: The adoption of digital payments has surged across the country, driven by convenience and government promotion. This trend is fostering a less-cash economy and facilitating financial inclusion.

articles.headline: Oracle India begins massive layoffs affecting estimated 10,000 employees
articles.summary: Oracle India has initiated a significant workforce reduction, with an estimated 10,000 employees losing their jobs as the company shifts focus to AI-driven cloud infrastructure. Staff reported being locked out of systems with no prior notice. Experts suggest the move is part of a global restructuring effort intended to save approximately $1.6 billion annually.

articles.headline: Zetwerk files confidential papers for ₹5,000 crore IPO
articles.summary: Manufacturing services startup Zetwerk has filed confidential draft papers with SEBI for an initial public offering (IPO) seeking to raise ₹5,000 crore. The Bengaluru-based company is reportedly looking for a valuation of around ₹25,000 crore. The proceeds will likely be used for global expansion and debt repayment, as the firm aims to capitalise on the move toward diversified supply chains.

articles.headline: Oracle to lay off 30,000 employees globally in AI pivot
articles.summary: Technology major Oracle Corp. is cutting 19% of its workforce, impacting 30,000 roles worldwide, including an estimated 12,000-15,000 in India. The layoffs target sales, HR, and engineering roles as the firm shifts resources toward AI infrastructure. Experts suggest more SaaS companies may follow suit as they reallocate spending from employee costs to AI-driven capital expenditure.

articles.headline: Self-reliance Inching Up for Key Electronics Gear in India
articles.summary: Approved investments in local electronics components manufacturing are expected to meet 100% of India’s domestic demands for key gear like copper clad laminates. The Electronic Component Manufacturing Scheme (ECMS) has greenlit 75 proposals across 12 states, with production for critical passive components expected by 2026-end.

articles.headline: Anthropic Leaks Part of Claude AI Code Due to Human Error
articles.summary: Anthropic inadvertently released source code for its Claude AI agent, compromising approximately 1,900 files and 512,000 lines of code. The company attributed the incident to a "release packaging issue caused by human error, not a security breach," assuring no sensitive customer data or credentials were exposed.

articles.headline: Siri May Soon Process Multiple Tasks at Once in OS 27
articles.summary: Apple is testing a feature for Siri that would enable it to process multiple requests in a single query. This capability is expected to be part of the upcoming iOS 27 operating systems, breaking new ground for the nearly 15-year-old digital assistant.

articles.headline: Samsung Galaxy Watches to Get Blood Pressure Tracking
articles.summary: Samsung plans to introduce a blood pressure monitoring tool for Galaxy Watch 4 and newer models. Users will need to download the Samsung Health Monitor app and complete an initial calibration to track their blood pressure.

articles.headline: Google Tests Screenless BP Tracking Fitbit Band
articles.summary: Alphabet's Google is developing its own screenless wearable device under the Fitbit brand for blood pressure tracking. The device, aimed at competing with upstarts like Whoop and Oura Health, is planned for release later this year.

articles.headline: Plagiarist’s Human, Not AI!: A Take on AI and Plagiarism
articles.summary: The opinion piece argues that recent plagiarism scandals linked to AI, like a freelance writer using AI for a book review, misplace blame. It asserts that plagiarism is a uniquely human act of intellectual theft, and AI merely generates content from its training data, without intent. The human editor, not the AI, is responsible for screening original content.

articles.headline: ‘Silent’ Tech May Edge Out SMS OTPs Soon for Banking Security
articles.summary: Top private banks and telcos are developing ‘silent authentication’ to replace SMS OTPs. This background check verifies if a banking app's mobile number matches the active SIM, flagging discrepancies in real-time. It aims to eliminate fraud from SIM cloning and eSIM swaps, aligning with RBI’s 2FA mandate.

articles.headline: DoT Extends Deadline for OTT App SIM-Binding to December 31
articles.summary: The Department of Telecommunications (DoT) extended the deadline for OTT platforms to implement SIM-binding to December 31, 2026, following industry pushback. It also rolled back the 6-hour auto-logout mandate for web/desktop sessions, adopting a risk-based approach for suspected fraud cases.

articles.headline: SpaceX Execs Meet Scindia as Starlink Awaits India Clearance
articles.summary: SpaceX's senior leadership, including President Gwynne Shotwell and Starlink VP Lauren Dreyer, met Telecom Minister Jyotiraditya Scindia as the company awaits regulatory approvals for India services. Starlink has signed letters of intent with Meghalaya to pilot satellite internet in remote regions.

articles.headline: Anthropic addresses accidental leak of Claude Code internal source code
articles.summary: AI startup Anthropic is working to mitigate an inadvertent release of source code for "Claude Code," its AI-powered developer tool. The company removed thousands of copies from GitHub and attributed the leak to a manual step failure in its deployment process. No sensitive customer data was exposed, but the incident follows a previous storage slip-up involving internal files.

articles.headline: Apple celebrates 50 years, from garage dream to global powerhouse
articles.summary: Apple, founded in 1976 by Steve Wozniak and Steve Jobs, celebrates 50 years, transforming technology with desktop computers, smartphones, and mobile apps. CEO Tim Cook emphasizes innovation, human-centric design, and customer trust. The company now faces increasing pressure to maintain leadership in the era of artificial intelligence.

articles.headline: Oracle India layoffs hit thousands, raises AI concern
articles.summary: Oracle reportedly laid off thousands of employees in India (est. 2,500-3,000, globally 30,000) due to "broader organisational change" and a strategic shift towards AI-ready services. This move, driven by Chairman Larry Ellison's focus on building AI data centres, is seen as a harsh reminder of AI's impact on tech jobs.

articles.headline: Wipro names Nagendra Bandaru AI chief, Americas 2 CEO resigns
articles.summary: Wipro appointed Nagendra Bandaru as CEO of its new AI segment, focusing on agentic AI solutions. Concurrently, Suzanne Dann, CEO of Wipro's "Americas 2" unit (one-third of revenue), resigned.

articles.headline: Refurbished phones gain market share as new prices rise
articles.summary: India's smartphone market expects a rising share of refurbished devices as new phone prices surged 15-20% (up to 30% for some models) due to quadrupled memory costs. Shipments are projected to contract 10% in 2026. Consumers are shifting to value options like refurbished iPhones.

articles.headline: AI inferencing to drive India's data centre growth
articles.summary: India's data centre business growth will be led by AI inference companies, not just LLM training, states Rajesh Tapadia of Iron Mountain. The company plans to add 150 Mw capacity in three years, leveraging new, AI-ready buildings and surplus green power availability in India.

articles.headline: DoT may extend SIM-binding deadline to December-end
articles.summary: The Department of Telecommunications (DoT) may extend the SIM-binding deadline for OTT platforms like WhatsApp and Telegram until December-end, citing technical challenges. The directive, aimed at curbing fraud, requires KYC-verified SIMs for app-based communication services.

articles.headline: Two-factor authentication operational for all digital transactions
articles.summary: Two-factor authentication (2FA) became effective for all digital transactions, including UPI, from Wednesday, as per RBI directions. Users now need an OTP, fingerprint, or facial recognition in addition to their PIN. This aims to reduce bank fraud and improve accountability. Screenshots in banking apps are banned.

articles.headline: Mobile PLI investments double, exports hit ~2.62 trn
articles.summary: India's production-linked incentive (PLI) scheme for mobile phone manufacturing attracted ~17,519 crore, double the target. Total production reached ~8.12 trillion (36% above target). In 2025, smartphone exports crossed ~2.62 trillion, making them India’s top exported commodity.

articles.headline: Nvidia's mcap nears India's total equity market
articles.summary: Nvidia Corp's market capitalization (mcap) of $4.24 trillion is rapidly converging with India's entire equity market mcap of $4.37 trillion, driven by the AI boom. A year ago, Nvidia's value was less than half of India's. India's mcap declined ~20% recently due to FPI outflows.

articles.headline: Oracle cuts 12,000 India jobs in global AI-led restructuring drive
articles.summary: Oracle is cutting around 12,000 jobs in India, representing roughly 40% of its global reduction of 30,000 employees, as part of an AI infrastructure push. The layoffs span various functions and roles, with affected employees informed via email without prior one-on-one discussions. Severance benefits are provided, aligning with company policy.

articles.headline: Wipro announces AI-Native business, reshuffles leadership
articles.summary: Wipro announced a new AI-Native Business & Platforms unit, aiming to deliver consulting-led, AI-powered transformation for clients. Nagendra Bandaru, President and Managing Partner of Technology Services GBL, has been appointed CEO of this new unit. Suzanne Dann, CEO for Wipro’s Americas-2 Strategic Market Unit, stepped down.

articles.headline: NOTHING PHONE (4A) PRO. Standout design and a complete package
articles.summary: The Nothing Phone (4a) Pro offers a unique all-metal unibody chassis in three finishes, including Silver, and is the thinnest full-metal phone at 7.95 mm. It features a 6.83-inch AMOLED display with 1.5K resolution and a 144 Hz refresh rate, a 50 MP triple-camera system, and is powered by the Qualcomm Snapdragon 7 Gen 4 processor.

articles.headline: YouTube Premium Lite gets background play, downloads
articles.summary: YouTube’s Premium Lite subscription service will now include background play and downloads, rolling out in India in the coming weeks. This update aims to enhance the user experience, allowing subscribers to watch most videos ad-free, offline, and in the background, building on feedback from its pilot program.

articles.headline: Samsung, Apple phones can now share files with AirDrop
articles.summary: Samsung Electronics Co’s latest smartphones, including the Galaxy S26 series, can now share photos and videos with Apple Inc’s iPhone owners using the built-in Quick Share feature and AirDrop. This marks a new level of compatibility between industry rivals, without Apple’s direct involvement in developing the solution.

articles.headline: WhatsApp rolls out updates to enhance user experience
articles.summary: WhatsApp has introduced a set of updates focused on storage management, cross-platform chat transfers, multi-account support on iOS, and new AI-driven tools within chats. Users can now identify and delete large files directly, migrate chats between iOS and Android, and use AI-powered features like in-chat photo editing.

articles.headline: Telecom infra must rest on strong fibre network
articles.summary: India's digital economy relies heavily on mobile networks, an imbalance compared to advanced economies with deep fixed broadband infrastructure. This mobile-first trajectory, while democratizing access, creates network strain and limits productivity for advanced applications. Rebalancing infrastructure priorities towards a 'fibre first' approach is crucial for long-term digital capital formation and resilience.

articles.headline: Capacity of data centres to grow 30% in 2026’
articles.summary: India’s total data centre (DC) stock is projected to increase by 30% year-on-year in 2026, supported by an estimated 500 MW of new supply. Investments in the sector totalled $56.4 billion in 2025, bringing the cumulative total to $126 billion. Data centres are now classified as a mainstream asset class, attracting significant foreign capital.

articles.headline: Artificial intelligence start-ups dominate VC interest even as total funding slumps
articles.summary: Artificial intelligence-led start-ups continued to dominate investor attention in FY26, even as overall funding activity moderated by 9% to $10.1 billion across 977 deals. The generative AI wave reshaped early-stage investing, intensifying competition, and pushing investors towards more specialized, vertical AI applications with a focus on real usage and problem-solving depth.

articles.headline: Technology Innovations Showcase
articles.summary: An event showcased the latest technology innovations, highlighting breakthroughs in artificial intelligence and digital transformation. Startups presented their solutions for various societal challenges.

articles.headline: Cybersecurity Measures Strengthened
articles.summary: Cybersecurity measures across government and private sectors have been strengthened in response to evolving digital threats. New protocols and technologies are being implemented to protect data.

articles.headline: Technological Advancements in Agriculture
articles.summary: Technological advancements in agriculture are being promoted to improve crop yields and farming efficiency. Smart farming solutions and genetic research are key areas of focus for sustainable practices.

articles.headline: AI Integration in Public Sector
articles.summary: The integration of Artificial Intelligence in the public sector is being explored to enhance service delivery and operational efficiency. Pilot projects are underway in key government departments.

articles.headline: Broadband Connectivity for All
articles.summary: The government launched a program to provide universal broadband connectivity, aiming to connect remote villages. This initiative is expected to boost digital literacy and economic opportunities in rural areas.

articles.headline: Blockchain Technology Adoption
articles.summary: The adoption of blockchain technology is being explored in various sectors beyond finance, including supply chain management and healthcare. Pilot projects are testing its potential for transparency and security.

articles.headline: Robotics in Manufacturing Sector
articles.summary: The manufacturing sector is increasingly adopting robotics to enhance productivity and precision. Automation is transforming factory floors, leading to discussions on future workforce adaptation and skills.

articles.headline: Augmented Reality Applications
articles.summary: The development of augmented reality applications is expanding into various fields, including education, healthcare, and retail. These innovative technologies promise enhanced user experiences and efficiency.

articles.headline: Cloud Computing Adoption Grows
articles.summary: The adoption of cloud computing services is growing rapidly across industries, offering scalable and flexible IT infrastructure. This trend is driving digital transformation and efficiency improvements for businesses.

articles.headline: Data Analytics in Governance
articles.summary: The application of data analytics in governance is improving policy-making and service delivery. Government agencies are using data-driven insights to optimize public resources and enhance efficiency.

articles.headline: Cybersecurity in Financial Services
articles.summary: Cybersecurity measures in financial services are being reinforced to protect against cyber threats and data breaches. Banks and financial institutions are investing in advanced security technologies.

articles.headline: Smart Manufacturing Technologies
articles.summary: The adoption of smart manufacturing technologies is transforming industries, enhancing productivity and efficiency. Automation, IoT, and AI are key components of this industrial revolution.

articles.headline: IoT Adoption in Industries
articles.summary: The adoption of Internet of Things (IoT) solutions is increasing across industries, driving automation and data-driven decision-making. Smart sensors and connected devices are transforming operational efficiency.

articles.headline: AI in Healthcare Diagnostics
articles.summary: The application of Artificial Intelligence in healthcare diagnostics is enhancing accuracy and efficiency. AI-powered tools are assisting doctors in early disease detection and personalized treatment plans.

articles.headline: Robotics in Service Industry
articles.summary: Robotics is increasingly being adopted in the service industry, from hospitality to logistics, improving efficiency and customer experience. Automation is transforming traditional service roles.

articles.headline: AI in Education Sector
articles.summary: The integration of AI in the education sector is personalizing learning experiences and automating administrative tasks. AI-powered tools are helping educators tailor content and assess student progress more effectively.

articles.headline: Indian Railways achieves record 1,670 million tonnes freight in FY26
articles.summary: Indian Railways transported a record 1,670 million tonnes of freight in the fiscal year ended March 2026, a 3.25% increase over the previous year. The performance was supported by a 4.56% rise in the number of wagons handled. The growth reinforces the railways' role as the backbone of national logistics despite global supply chain disruptions.

articles.headline: Power consumption grows marginally in March due to unseasonal rains
articles.summary: India's power consumption rose by 1.8% to 149.56 billion units in March. Intermittent rains across the country delayed the onset of summer, leading to lower use of cooling appliances like air-conditioners. Peak power demand met during the month rose slightly to 238.37 GW. The modest growth is expected to surge as temperatures rise in the coming months.

articles.headline: India data centre segment to attract $54 billion investment
articles.summary: Real estate consultant CBRE projects that India's data centre stock across major cities will grow 30% year-on-year in 2026. The segment is expected to see investment commitments exceeding $54 billion this year, with an estimated 500MW of new supply addition. The growth is fueled by increasing digitalization and corporate demand for data storage infrastructure.

articles.headline: Private airports generate over 30% of AAI’s net profit
articles.summary: Six airports operated under public-private partnerships, including those run by Adani, contributed over 30% of the Airports Authority of India's (AAI) net profit in FY25. Conversely, 75% of AAI's own 122 airports reported losses totaling ₹1,600 crore. The data highlights AAI's shift toward a landlord model where lease rentals from private operators sustain its earnings.

articles.headline: Railways Clocks 3.2% Increase in FY26 Freight Loading
articles.summary: Indian Railways recorded 1,670 million tonnes of freight loading in FY26, a 3.25% annual increase, driven by strong demand for industrial commodities. Freight earnings rose to ₹1.78 lakh crore, with fertiliser, pig iron, finished steel, and iron ore transport leading growth. Passenger numbers and revenue also increased.

articles.headline: LPG tanker Pine Gas rerouted to Vizag for quicker cargo turnaround
articles.summary: The India-flagged LPG tanker Pine Gas, carrying 47,000 tonnes of cooking fuel from the Persian Gulf, is expected to dock at Vizag port on Thursday. Originally destined for Dhamra or New Mangalore, the ship was rerouted to ensure faster discharge. Vizag port has been prioritized for LPG handling as the government seeks to secure domestic supplies amid disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.headline: Railways freight grows 3.3% in FY26; coal cargo contracts
articles.summary: Indian Railways ferried 1,670 million tonnes of cargo in FY26, a 3.3% Y-o-Y growth but 30mt short of target. Coal cargo, its mainstay, contracted 0.5% Y-o-Y. Freight earnings reached ~1,77,754 crore. Fertilizers and pig iron/steel saw the highest growth.

articles.headline: A BRIDGE TO AMARAVATI
articles.summary: A steel bridge is under rapid construction over Kondaveeti Vagu, a stream, as part of the Amaravati Seed Access Road and the N4 road project in Andhra Pradesh. The bridge is essential for improving connectivity between Vijayawada, Guntur, Mangalagiri, and the Amaravati capital region, with completion expected to significantly enhance transportation infrastructure.

articles.headline: Updates on Public Services Expansion
articles.summary: Updates were provided on the expansion of public services in urban areas, focusing on efficiency and accessibility. New facilities and improved delivery mechanisms are expected to benefit residents.

articles.headline: New City Development Plans Unveiled
articles.summary: New development plans for smart cities were unveiled, focusing on sustainable urban growth and improved public amenities. The project aims to enhance living standards and promote economic opportunities.

articles.headline: Digital Infrastructure Expansion Planned
articles.summary: Plans for expanding digital infrastructure in rural areas were unveiled, aiming to bridge the digital divide. This initiative promises improved internet access and digital literacy for remote communities.

articles.headline: Infrastructure Development Projects
articles.summary: Several major infrastructure development projects are progressing as planned, focusing on transportation networks and energy supply. These projects are expected to stimulate regional economic growth.

articles.headline: Rural Infrastructure Projects Progress
articles.summary: Progress reports on rural infrastructure projects show significant headway in road construction and water supply initiatives. These developments are crucial for connectivity and local development.

articles.headline: National Highway Project Inaugurated
articles.summary: A major section of the national highway project was inaugurated, promising enhanced connectivity and reduced travel times. The infrastructure upgrade is expected to boost trade and tourism in the region.

articles.headline: Urban Planning for Sustainable Growth
articles.summary: New urban planning strategies are being implemented to ensure sustainable growth and manage increasing population density. These plans focus on green spaces, efficient transport, and public amenities.

articles.headline: New Public Transport Systems
articles.summary: New public transport systems are being developed in major urban centers to ease congestion and improve commuter experience. The projects include metro expansions and modern bus networks.

articles.headline: Renewable Energy Parks Planned
articles.summary: New renewable energy parks are planned across several states, focusing on solar and wind power generation. These projects are part of the national commitment to sustainable energy and carbon reduction goals.

articles.headline: Port Modernization Project Advances
articles.summary: The port modernization project is advancing rapidly, aiming to enhance trade capacities and logistics efficiency. This infrastructure upgrade is crucial for international commerce and economic competitiveness.

articles.headline: High-Speed Rail Project Update
articles.summary: An update on the high-speed rail project revealed significant progress in construction and land acquisition. The ambitious infrastructure project promises to transform inter-city travel and connectivity.

articles.headline: Smart Grid Project Initiated
articles.summary: A smart grid project has been initiated to modernize the power distribution network, ensuring reliable and efficient electricity supply. This infrastructure upgrade leverages advanced digital technologies.

articles.headline: Urban Transport Network Expansion
articles.summary: Plans for expanding the urban transport network include new metro lines and dedicated bus corridors. These projects aim to ease traffic congestion and improve connectivity for commuters in major cities.

articles.headline: Rural Electrification Project Success
articles.summary: The rural electrification project has achieved significant success, bringing electricity to thousands of remote villages. This infrastructure development is transforming lives and stimulating local economies.

articles.headline: Logistics and Supply Chain Upgrades
articles.summary: Major upgrades to logistics and supply chain infrastructure are improving efficiency and reducing costs for businesses. New warehouses, freight corridors, and digital tracking systems are key components.

articles.headline: Urban Drainage System Upgrades
articles.summary: Urban drainage systems are undergoing significant upgrades to prevent flooding during monsoon seasons. These infrastructure improvements are critical for urban resilience and public safety.

articles.headline: Rural Connectivity Projects
articles.summary: Rural connectivity projects are making significant progress, building new roads and bridges to link remote villages. These infrastructure developments are crucial for economic growth and social inclusion.

articles.headline: Infrastructure Development in Northeast
articles.summary: Infrastructure development in the Northeast region is a priority, with new road, rail, and air connectivity projects. These initiatives aim to integrate the region into the national mainstream and boost economy.

articles.headline: National Road Network Expansion
articles.summary: The national road network expansion project is proceeding on schedule, aiming to improve connectivity across states. New highways and expressways will boost trade and reduce travel times.

articles.headline: BDA launches six new housing projects to build 4,251 flats
articles.summary: The Bangalore Development Authority (BDA) has announced six new housing projects in various parts of the city, including Kommaghatta and Gunjur. The projects aim to create 4,251 flats ranging from 1BHK to 3BHK to meet the growing demand for urban housing. With a total investment of ₹3,200 crore, the BDA intends to provide affordable vertical housing options with integrated infrastructure.

articles.headline: CAG flags dilapidated condition of Indian Embassy building in Beijing
articles.summary: The Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) has criticized the "extremely dilapidated condition" of the Indian Embassy’s new building in Beijing. The report highlights severe structural issues, including water leaks and poor maintenance, despite significant expenditure on renovation. The CAG called for a thorough investigation into the project's execution and financial oversight by officials.

articles.headline: Supreme Court admits steel firms’ plea on coke breeze duty
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has agreed to hear a dispute regarding customs duty exemptions for 'coke breeze,' a steelmaking input. Steelmakers like Jindal Steel are challenging the customs department's decision to tax coke breeze at 5% while metallurgical coke is exempt. The ruling will determine classification and have significant cost implications for the iron and steel industry.

articles.headline: Tribunals Can Accept Fresh Documents in SIR Appeals After Authenticity Check
articles.summary: The Supreme Court allowed appellate tribunals in West Bengal to accept new evidence for electoral roll appeals, provided authenticity is verified. The ECI assured tribunals access to officials for inclusion/exclusion, aiming to clear pending claims by April 7. This follows a Trinamool Congress plea against bulk Form 6 applications.

articles.headline: FM: CoC Can Invite Land Authorities in Realty Insolvency Cases
articles.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman announced that the Committee of Creditors (CoC) in insolvency cases can invite land authorities to meetings for inputs on real estate projects. This aims to expedite bankruptcy resolution for hundreds of realty projects, noting 111 cases involving 1.6 lakh homebuyers are resolved, with 210 more under resolution.

articles.headline: GAAR Shadow Over Payments Abroad Worries India Inc
articles.summary: Indian companies and banks are seeking clarity on the General Anti-Avoidance Rules (GAAR) applicability to payments like dividends, interest, and royalties to foreign shareholders. They fear the tax department might use GAAR to deny treaty benefits, especially after the Supreme Court's Tiger Global ruling, demanding better safeguards for payers.

articles.headline: Door’s Open, Yet Foreign Law Cos Stay on the Fence in India
articles.summary: Nearly a year after India liberalized its legal market, foreign law firms remain hesitant to open offices due to regulatory uncertainty from BCI, tax ambiguity, and thin margins. While BCI rules allow advising on M&A, IP, and contracts, firms cannot appear in courts or handle property conveyancing, and face strong competition.

articles.headline: SC Dismisses PIL Seeking AI Crash Report Details
articles.summary: The Supreme Court dismissed an appeal seeking detailed disclosure of a preliminary report on the June 12 crash of a London-bound Air India flight in Ahmedabad. The Chief Justice questioned the petitioner's agenda, as victims' families had not approached the court, criticizing the filing of such PILs.

articles.headline: Paramvah Studios Ordered to Pay ``25 Lakh to MRT Music
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court ordered Paramvah Studios, Rakshit Shetty's production house, to pay ₹25 lakh to MRT Music for unauthorised use of two songs in its Kannada film 'Bachelor Party'. The court found the studio liable for copyright infringement and wilful disobedience of an earlier order.

articles.headline: SC allows tribunals to hear appeals in Bengal voter revision exercise
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that voters in West Bengal can appeal to specialized tribunals during the Special Intensive Revision exercise. 19 tribunals headed by former judges will examine documents for those excluded from the rolls. The court emphasized that the right to vote cannot be "washed out" through oppressive situations, though only names cleared by nomination dates can vote.

articles.headline: Karnataka Nursing Council registrar arrested for alleged sexual harassment
articles.summary: K Mallu, the registrar at the Karnataka State Nursing Council, was arrested and remanded to 14-day custody for allegedly sexually harassing a 25-year-old colleague. The victim detailed multiple instances of inappropriate behavior at the council's Gandhinagar office. Police are examining CCTV footage and staff statements to gather evidence following the formal complaint.

articles.headline: Karnataka reports 468 bomb hoax threats over last six years
articles.summary: Data from the state home department shows Karnataka has faced 468 hoax bomb threats since 2020, with 90% occurring in Bengaluru. Police have formed a dedicated investigation team to trace perpetrators using assistance from Interpol. Only 14 cases have been solved so far. Most threats targeted schools, airports, and embassies through emails suspected to originate from abroad.

articles.headline: Supreme Court stays corruption trial against Tamil Nadu minister Durai Murugan
articles.summary: The Supreme Court stayed trial court proceedings against Tamil Nadu minister and DMK leader Durai Murugan in a disproportionate assets case. A bench issued a notice to the state government after Murugan challenged a Madras High Court order. The stay will remain in effect until the next hearing on April 20 as the court examines the legality of the high court's decision.

articles.headline: Man arrested for sending obscene messages to Kerala Women's Commission head
articles.summary: A 37-year-old man from Kannur was arrested for allegedly sending obscene images and messages to the State Women's Commission chairperson. The accused, Puthanveetil Jagadeesh, was remanded to judicial custody after a complaint was filed under the IT Act and BNS. Police are investigating the nature of the harassment directed at the commission head.

articles.headline: Union Minister Suresh Gopi to face trial over election malpractice allegations
articles.summary: The Kerala High Court ruled that an election petition against BJP MP Suresh Gopi is maintainable, requiring him to face trial for alleged corrupt practices. The petition filed by an AIYF leader claims Gopi used religious symbols and promised gifts during the Thrissur Lok Sabha campaign. Gopi plans to challenge the ruling in higher courts.

articles.headline: Director Ranjith remanded to judicial custody in sexual assault case
articles.summary: Noted Malayalam filmmaker Ranjith Balakrishnan was sent to 14 days of judicial custody following his arrest for allegedly assaulting an actress in 2024. The arrest followed an SIT investigation into claims that the assault occurred inside a caravan on a film set. Ranjith, who denies the charges, has been suspended from various film unions following the legal action.

articles.headline: NIA summons TMC candidate Manab Kumar Porua in 2022 blast case
articles.summary: The National Investigation Agency summoned TMC candidate Manab Kumar Porua for questioning regarding a December 2022 bomb explosion in East Midnapore that killed three people. Porua, contesting from Bhagabanpur, alleged the notice was served to disrupt his campaign. The TMC termed the move "politically motivated," while the BJP maintained the NIA is following a court order.

articles.headline: Rajya Sabha passes CAPF Bill to unify force administration and recruitment
articles.summary: The Rajya Sabha passed the Central Armed Police Forces (General Administration) Bill, 2026, despite an Opposition walkout. The law establishes a unified legal framework for all five CAPFs and formalizes IPS deputation quotas for senior ranks. The government claims it will boost morale and efficiency, while the Opposition argues it restricts career progression for cadre officers.

articles.headline: Bombay High Court warns Republic TV over Anil Ambani investigation coverage
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court orally cautioned Republic TV and Arnab Goswami to avoid "hitting below the belt" in their coverage of ED investigations linked to Anil Ambani. The court was hearing a defamation suit filed by Ambani regarding reports linking him to alleged loan fraud at RCom. The judge directed the channel to refrain from using "superlative taglines" against individuals.

articles.headline: Delhi court convicts Madhya Pradesh Congress MLA Rajendra Bharti for cheating
articles.summary: A Delhi court convicted MP Congress MLA Rajendra Bharti and a former cashier for criminal conspiracy, cheating, and forgery of bank records. The case involved drawing unauthorized high-interest payments from a cooperative bank for a family trust. Bharti, who served as the bank's chairman during the fraud, will face sentencing on Thursday.

articles.headline: SC to hear Anil Ambani's plea against 'fraud' tag
articles.summary: The Supreme Court will hear Anil Ambani's plea next week challenging banks' "fraud" classification for his accounts, related to alleged ~31,580 crore irregularities in Reliance Communications. CBI registered a new case against Ambani and RCom for allegedly causing ~3,750 crore loss to LIC through false representations for NCDs.

articles.headline: Parliament passes IBC Bill; FM highlights land authority CoC participation
articles.summary: Parliament passed the IBC Bill, allowing land authorities to join Committee of Creditors (CoC) meetings for inputs on regulatory/land development. Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman said 111 out of 565 real estate cases in IBC were resolved, benefiting 50,000 homebuyers, and stressed on project-wise resolution plans.

articles.headline: Structural reforms needed in legal profession for gender equality
articles.summary: A Supreme Court Bar Association survey shows over 80% of women lawyers find the profession tough, facing gender bias, lack of mentorship, unequal pay, and networking barriers. Women are only 15% of lawyers and 2% of Bar Council leaders. Structural reforms are needed for a balanced judiciary, where only one of 33 SC judges is a woman.

articles.headline: CBDT amends GAAR rules to restore tax certainty after Tiger Global ruling
articles.summary: The CBDT has amended Income-Tax Rules, clarifying that investments made by private equity and hedge funds before April 1, 2017, will not be subject to additional tax under GAAR. This resolves uncertainty stemming from the recent Supreme Court ruling in the Tiger Global case on Flipkart shares, restoring the original grandfathering intent.

articles.headline: Parliamentary committee picks holes in SHANTI legislation
articles.summary: A Parliamentary committee has criticized the recently passed SHANTI Act, designed to facilitate private sector participation in nuclear energy. Concerns were raised regarding the removal of supplier liability, 'restricted information' provisions, and the Act's silence on radioactive waste management policy, recommending statutory independent auditing and clearer policy frameworks.

articles.headline: New Regulatory Framework Under Consultation
articles.summary: A new regulatory framework is under consultation, aiming to streamline processes and ensure compliance across industries. Feedback from businesses and civil society groups is being gathered for refinement.

articles.headline: Judicial Reforms on the Agenda
articles.summary: Judicial reforms are on the agenda, with discussions focusing on speeding up court processes and improving access to justice. Legal experts are providing input on proposed changes.

articles.headline: Judicial Decisions on Property Disputes
articles.summary: Recent judicial decisions have provided clarity on complex property disputes, impacting real estate development and land ownership. Legal experts are analyzing the implications for future cases.

articles.headline: Legal Framework for Digital Assets
articles.summary: A new legal framework for digital assets and cryptocurrencies is being developed to regulate the growing sector. The aim is to ensure investor protection and prevent illicit financial activities.

articles.headline: Consumer Protection Law Changes
articles.summary: Changes to consumer protection laws aim to provide greater safeguards for buyers and ensure fair trade practices. New regulations address online fraud and product quality standards.

articles.headline: Consumer Data Protection Laws
articles.summary: New consumer data protection laws are being enacted to safeguard personal information in the digital age. The regulations aim to enhance privacy rights and ensure responsible data handling by corporations.

articles.headline: Property Tax Reforms Implemented
articles.summary: Property tax reforms have been implemented to rationalize municipal revenues and streamline collection processes. The changes aim to ensure fairness and efficiency in urban local bodies.

articles.headline: Legal Challenge to Government Order
articles.summary: A legal challenge has been filed against a recent government order, raising questions about its constitutional validity. The Supreme Court is expected to hear arguments in the coming weeks.

articles.headline: Corporate Law Amendments Proposed
articles.summary: Amendments to corporate law are proposed to enhance corporate governance and investor protection. The changes aim to create a more robust and transparent business environment for domestic and foreign entities.

articles.headline: Family Law Reforms Proposed
articles.summary: Reforms in family law are proposed to address contemporary societal needs and ensure equitable justice. Discussions involve legal experts and civil society organizations for comprehensive changes.

articles.headline: Electoral Law Amendments
articles.summary: Amendments to electoral laws are being debated to ensure fair and transparent elections. Proposals include changes to campaign finance regulations and voter registration processes.

articles.headline: Cybercrime Legislation Updates
articles.summary: Updates to cybercrime legislation are being considered to address evolving digital threats and online fraud. The new laws aim to enhance enforcement capabilities and protect citizens from cyber attacks.

articles.headline: Judicial Appointments Announced
articles.summary: New judicial appointments were announced to fill vacancies in various courts, strengthening the justice delivery system. The appointments aim to expedite case resolution and enhance judicial efficiency.

articles.headline: Arbitration Law Reforms
articles.summary: Reforms in arbitration law are aimed at expediting commercial dispute resolution and promoting alternative mechanisms. The changes seek to enhance ease of doing business and investor confidence.

articles.headline: Consumer Rights Protection
articles.summary: Enhanced consumer rights protection measures are being introduced to safeguard interests in online transactions and digital services. Regulations aim for transparency and accountability from service providers.

articles.headline: Legal Reforms for Business Ease
articles.summary: New legal reforms are aimed at improving the ease of doing business, simplifying regulations and reducing compliance burdens. These changes seek to attract investment and stimulate economic activity.

articles.headline: US Supreme Court reviews birthright citizenship for children of immigrants
articles.summary: The US Supreme Court heard arguments regarding birthright citizenship for children born in the US to undocumented immigrants. The case could redefine the interpretation of the 14th Amendment. The Trump administration argues citizenship should not be automatic if parents are not under US jurisdiction. A final ruling on the matter is expected by the end of the summer term.

articles.headline: CBI files fresh FIR against Anil Ambani in LIC fraud case
articles.summary: The Central Bureau of Investigation registered a new FIR against industrialist Anil Ambani and former bank officials for alleged fraud involving a ₹3,750 crore loan from LIC. The complaint alleges that funds intended for infrastructure projects were diverted through shell companies. The case follows a forensic audit that flagged financial irregularities and misrepresentation of assets.

articles.headline: HC allows prosecution of IAS officer Rohini Sindhuri in bag scam
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court has cleared the way for the prosecution of IAS officer Rohini Sindhuri regarding alleged irregularities in the procurement of eco-friendly bags. The court directed the state government to reconsider the grant of sanction for prosecution under the Prevention of Corruption Act. The case involves a ₹7.5-crore loss to the state exchequer during her tenure.

articles.headline: SC directs Bengal to decide 60 lakh voter claims by April 7
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has ordered the West Bengal government to complete the verification of 60 lakh claims for inclusion in the voters' list by April 7, 2026. A bench led by Justice Surya Kant noted that 47.3 lakh claims have already been processed. The court emphasized that no legitimate voter should be left out of the electoral rolls before the upcoming assembly elections in the state.

articles.headline: Agra court awards death to man for killing daughter-in-law
articles.summary: An Agra court has awarded the death penalty to a man and his son for the brutal murder of their daughter-in-law in 2022. The judge described the crime as falling into the "rarest of rare" categories, noting the extreme cruelty involved. The victim was killed following a dispute over domestic issues and dowry demands. The convicts were also fined ₹50,000 each by the court.

articles.headline: UAE seeks military coalition to force open Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: The United Arab Emirates is lobbying for a UN resolution to authorize the use of force to open the Strait of Hormuz. The Gulf state fears permanent Iranian oversight of the waterway, which handles 20% of global oil trade. The move marks a fundamental shift in the UAE's strategic posture, signaling its willingness to become a combatant to secure vital energy routes.

articles.headline: Iran fires missiles across West Asia as US signals exit
articles.summary: Iran launched missile strikes targeting Israel, Bahrain, Kuwait, and the UAE as tensions escalated in the region. The attacks highlight the ongoing threat to global shipping and energy supplies. Meanwhile, US President Donald Trump suggested a potential early withdrawal from the conflict, stating the US has accomplished goals like preventing Iran from obtaining nuclear weapons.

articles.headline: UK Pushes to Open Hormuz Amid Iran War Threat
articles.summary: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced a diplomatic push to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, coordinating with 35 nations. Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper will chair a meeting of G7, Europe, and Gulf nations to secure freedom of navigation and ensure vital commodity movement.

articles.headline: France, Japan Call for Ceasefire in Iran and Safe Shipping
articles.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron and Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi agreed on the urgent need for a ceasefire in Iran and safe passage for shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. Their meeting highlighted shared beliefs in international order amidst the crisis's impact on energy markets and global alliances.

articles.headline: US Judge Halts White House Ballroom Construction
articles.summary: A US judge ordered a halt to President Trump's massive White House ballroom construction, citing the need for congressional approval. The ruling came in response to a challenge from the National Trust for Historic Preservation, emphasizing the President's role as "steward" not "owner."

articles.headline: Bangladesh, Sri Lanka Seek US Waiver for Russian Fuel Imports
articles.summary: Bangladesh and Sri Lanka have requested a US waiver to import Russian fuel, following India's precedent. The move comes as the Iran war upends global energy markets, severely impacting smaller developing nations. Bangladesh proposes importing up to 600,000 tonnes of Russian diesel.

articles.headline: Afghanistan and Pakistan Talk Peace in Beijing
articles.summary: Pakistan and Afghanistan's Taliban governments are holding peace talks in Urumqi, China, following over a month of fighting. Officials from both countries are meeting, with Afghanistan's five-member delegation including representatives from the foreign, defense, and interior ministries.

articles.headline: Trump considers NATO exit and claims Iran requested a ceasefire
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump stated he is considering withdrawing from the NATO alliance and ending the war on Iran soon. He claimed Tehran requested a ceasefire, though Iran's Foreign Ministry denied this. Trump emphasized that US forces will exit once the Strait of Hormuz is reopened and free. Global oil supplies are expected to be hit hard as the US deploys more assets to the region.

articles.headline: Former PM Sheikh Hasina appeals against death sentence in Bangladesh
articles.summary: Sheikh Hasina has demanded that her death sentence, issued by the International Crimes Tribunal for crimes against humanity, be set aside as "legally void." In a legal communication, she called for any future proceedings to comply with international fair trial standards. Her lawyers cited "unlawful conduct of proceedings" during the trial following the 2024 crackdown on student protests.

articles.headline: Russian First Deputy PM Denis Manturov begins two-day India visit
articles.summary: Russian First Deputy Prime Minister Denis Manturov arrives in New Delhi for a two-day visit to discuss security, defense, and energy ties. He is scheduled to meet NSA Ajit Doval and External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar. The visit follows India's decision to acquire five more S-400 air defense systems and Russia becoming India's largest crude oil supplier in recent weeks.

articles.headline: Russian military plane crash kills 29 in Crimea
articles.summary: A Russian An-26 military transport plane crashed in annexed Crimea, killing six crew and 23 passengers. Separately, Russia claimed control of Luhansk region, which Ukraine denied, as Zelenskyy prepared for talks with US envoys.

articles.headline: UK to host summit on Hormuz deadlock
articles.summary: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced Britain would host meetings this week with allies to open the Strait of Hormuz, blocked by Iran since US-Israel conflict. 35 countries, including France, Germany, and Japan, will attend, focusing initially on mine-hunting.

articles.headline: Trump’s Iran war: escalation without end
articles.summary: The fifth week of Donald Trump’s illegal war on Iran has shown no overarching strategy, with continued US attacks on Iranian targets and retaliation from Iran and its proxies. The conflict has halted the Strait of Hormuz, causing oil price spikes and supply chain disruptions, suggesting a war of attrition with no imminent end or clear victory for either side.

articles.headline: International Trade Agreements Update

articles.headline: Trump suggests possible exit from Iran war and Nato alliance
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump stated he might end US involvement in the Iran war "very soon" and hinted at a potential withdrawal from the Nato alliance. Speaking at a rally, he claimed the US has been treated unfairly by partners and that military actions should have been swifter. Tensions in the Strait of Hormuz continue as the US military maintains a presence in the region.

articles.headline: Eli Lilly acquires Centessa for $7.8 billion to target narcolepsy
articles.summary: Eli Lilly & Co. is buying biotech firm Centessa Pharmaceuticals in a deal worth up to $7.8 billion to expand into sleep disorder treatments. Centessa is developing orexin receptor agonists that mimic brain neuropeptides to treat narcolepsy. The move fills a key portfolio gap for Lilly beyond its dominant obesity drugs, targeting a multi-billion-dollar market for wakefulness disorders.

articles.headline: NASA announces crew for historic Artemis II lunar flyby mission
articles.summary: Four astronauts—Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen—have been selected for the Artemis II mission, the first crewed lunar flight in over 50 years. Christina Koch will be the first woman and Victor Glover the first person of color to travel to the Moon. Jeremy Hansen will be the first non-American on a lunar mission, scheduled for late March 2026.

articles.headline: NASA to launch astronauts to Moon for first time in 50 years
articles.summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is scheduled to lift off from Kennedy Space Center on Wednesday at 22:24 GMT for a 10-day flight carrying four astronauts around the Moon. This is the first human mission to the Moon in 50 years, following delays due to technical issues.

articles.headline: Reorienting farm research
articles.summary: Indian agriculture has transformed from food deficit to surplus in 75 years, but now faces twin challenges: raising farmers’ incomes and ensuring sustainability through safe food production and environmental health. Agricultural research must evolve from nation-centric to farmer-centric, focusing on climate-resilient varieties, efficient resource use, value addition, and global competitiveness across diverse farming systems.

articles.headline: IPL 2026: Punjab Kings captain Shreyas Iyer fined for slow over-rate
articles.summary: Punjab Kings captain Shreyas Iyer has been fined ₹12 lakh after his team maintained a slow over-rate during their IPL 2026 match against Gujarat Titans. This was the first such offense for the team in the current season. Under IPL rules, the captain is penalized for failing to complete the required number of overs within the stipulated time during the match.

articles.headline: Tottenham Hotspur Football Club Roiled by Ownership Feud
articles.summary: Tottenham Hotspur football club faces a "season of disaster" amid an ownership feud between Daniel Levy and Joe Lewis, impacting its on-field performance. Lewis unceremoniously fired Levy, leading to potential lawsuits, while the club's future and sale prospects are uncertain, compounded by relegation threats.

articles.headline: Kerr’s Unbeaten 179 Powers New Zealand to Record ODI Chase
articles.summary: Amelia Kerr's unbeaten 179 off 139 balls led New Zealand to the highest successful run chase in women's ODI history, beating South Africa by two wickets. Chasing 347, her team surpassed India's previous record, marking her second-highest ODI score.

articles.headline: Sameer Rizvi’s Unbeaten 70 Powers DC to 6-Wicket IPL Win
articles.summary: Delhi Capitals recovered from a top-order collapse to secure a six-wicket IPL win over Lucknow Super Giants. Sameer Rizvi, named player of the match, hit an unbeaten 70 off 47 balls, sharing a crucial 119-run partnership with Tristan Stubbs (39*) to chase down Lucknow's 141 with 17 balls to spare.

articles.headline: India to Tour Zimbabwe for Three-T20I Series in July
articles.summary: India's men's team will tour Zimbabwe for a three-match T20I series in July, with all games scheduled at Harare Sports Club on July 23, 25, and 26. This marks the first of two bilateral engagements between the sides in the 2026–27 season.

articles.headline: Yamal Slams Anti-Muslim Chants in Spain-Egypt Game
articles.summary: Spain star Lamine Yamal condemned anti-Muslim chants made by Spanish fans during a friendly against Egypt, calling them "disrespectful and intolerable." Police are investigating the incident, which adds to Spain's ongoing issues with racist and sexist abuse in football.

articles.headline: KKR Seeks Spin Revival Against SRH in IPL Showdown
articles.summary: Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR) will aim for their spinners to regain form against Sunrisers Hyderabad (SRH) in tonight’s IPL clash at Eden Gardens. This match kicks off a crucial home stretch for KKR, playing three matches in seven days before a month-long break.

articles.headline: Uzbek prodigy Javokhir Sindarov takes sole lead in 2026 Candidates tournament
articles.summary: 20-year-old Javokhir Sindarov moved into the sole lead of the Candidates tournament after defeating world No. 3 Fabiano Caruana in Round 4. Sindarov now has three wins from four rounds. Meanwhile, India’s R Praggnanandhaa drew his match against Matthias Bluebaum, leaving him at two points. In the women's section, Divya Deshmukh suffered her first loss to top seed Zhu Jiner.

articles.headline: Punjab Kings assistant coach Brad Haddin praises Shreyas Iyer’s leadership
articles.summary: Brad Haddin, assistant coach of Punjab Kings, lauded captain Shreyas Iyer for his methodical leadership and calm demeanor. Iyer, who led the team to the finals last season, scored 604 runs in 2025 and has started the 2026 campaign with a win over GT. Haddin emphasized that Iyer’s world-class communication and preparation set a high standard for the entire group.

articles.headline: India U20 women’s football team faces Japan in Asian Cup opener
articles.summary: The India U20 women’s football team will begin its AFC Asian Cup campaign against Japan on Thursday. This marks India’s first qualification for the tournament in 20 years. Head coach Joakim Alexandersson acknowledged the challenge of facing the powerhouse Japanese side but expressed ambition to perform well on the continental stage.

articles.headline: Kaoru Mitoma scores as Japan secures historic 1-0 win over England
articles.summary: Japan achieved its first-ever victory against England with a 1-0 win at Wembley Stadium. Winger Kaoru Mitoma scored the decisive goal in the friendly match. The result marks a significant milestone for Hajime Moriyasu’s side, who had previously failed to beat England in three attempts.
