articles.headline: Karnataka to provide ₹15,000 monthly pension to former women cricketers
articles.summary: The Karnataka State Cricket Association (KSCA) announced a monthly pension of ₹15,000 for former women's national players from the state. To be eligible, players must have represented Karnataka in senior national championships and be at least 55 years old as of April 1, 2026. The move aims to provide financial security to veterans not covered under the BCCI national pension scheme.

articles.headline: Jain Premier League auction sees franchises sold for ₹31 crore
articles.summary: The inaugural Jain Premier League (JPL) cricket tournament concluded its auction with 12 franchises sold for a total of ₹31 crore. The Mumbai Sobo Star emerged as the most expensive team, bought for ₹3.7 crore. Over 50 players were auctioned for the T20 league, which aims to promote sports within the Jain community. The tournament is scheduled to begin in late April in Ahmedabad.

articles.headline: India likely to face Nigeria and Jamaica in UK cricket tour
articles.summary: The Indian men's national cricket team is likely to play Nigeria and Jamaica in a T20 series during their upcoming tour of the United Kingdom. While the schedule is yet to be finalized, the BCCI is in talks with the respective boards to organize these matches as part of a global outreach program. This would be the first time India faces Nigeria in a senior international cricket match.

articles.headline: Indian museums launch interactive after-hours sessions and date nights
articles.summary: Museums like the Museum of Art and Photography (MAP) and the Kiran Nadar Museum of Art are introducing evening programs to engage adult audiences. Offerings include storytelling workshops, dinner date nights, and immersive after-hours tours. These initiatives aim to transform museums from static repositories into pulsating, interactive social and creative spaces.

articles.headline: Nintendo releases sequel to Super Mario Bros movie
articles.summary: Shigeru Miyamoto has launched 'The Super Mario Galaxy Movie,' a sequel to the 2023 hit. The animated feature takes inspiration from the 2007 Wii game, focusing on deep space gravity mechanics. Nintendo has maintained tight control over the intellectual property to ensure character performance and visuals translate accurately from the gaming experience to the silver screen.

articles.headline: New book explores Kargil’s history through its culinary traditions
articles.summary: Yash Saxena’s new book, 'Stories from a Kargili Kitchen,' documents the food culture of the remote trans-Himalayan region. The work features 28 recipes and essays that trace the resilience of local communities through decades of conflict and trade. It highlights unique staples like skyu pasta soup and apricot-based dishes that have sustained the region's diverse populations.

articles.headline: Make Tirths Safe for All Kinds of Yatris
articles.summary: The article argues that India's religious tourism, despite its immense economic potential (projected $59bn by 2028), is marred by safety risks. Citing 4,000 stampedes in three decades, it advocates for universal safety measures and systematic crowd management, like the Golden Temple model, instead of exclusive access for the wealthy.

articles.headline: Football Titanic Tycoon’s Timepiece to Go on Auction
articles.summary: A Patek Philippe pocket watch, owned by John Jacob Astor IV and recovered from the Titanic, will be auctioned this month by Freeman’s. The 18-carat gold timepiece, estimated to fetch $300,000-$500,000, is a family heirloom and a rare historical piece linking Astor, Patek Philippe, and the Titanic.

articles.headline: Delhi government plans to rename Shahjahanabad Redevelopment Corporation
articles.summary: The Delhi government intends to rename the Shahjahanabad Redevelopment Corporation (SRDC), the body responsible for protecting Old Delhi's heritage. Names under consideration include Indraprastha Virasat Punarsthaapana Nigam and Indraprastha Dharohar Sanrakshan Evam Vikas Parishad. The move aligns with a broader push to emphasize the historical Indraprastha identity of the capital.

articles.headline: Annual Saleshwaram Jathara begins in Telangana’s Nallamala forest shrine
articles.summary: The three-day Saleshwaram Jathara, known as the "Dakshin Amarnath Yatra," commenced at the ancient cave shrine of Lord Shiva in Nagarkurnool. Thousands of devotees from Telangana and neighboring states are trekking through the Nallamala forest for the pilgrimage. The district administration has made arrangements for the event, which concludes on Chaitra Pournami.

articles.headline: Book Review: From Guerilla Fighter to Chief Minister: A Memoir
articles.summary: A review of Zoramthanga's memoir details his journey from Mizo National Front insurgent to Mizoram's Chief Minister. The book recounts Operation Jericho (IAF bombing of Aizawl in 1966), MNF's guerrilla tactics, and their complex negotiations for peace, leading to statehood for Mizoram in 1986.

articles.headline: Kerala Faces FCRA Hurdle: BJP’s Christian Outreach Impacted
articles.summary: BJP's outreach to Christians in Kerala faces hurdles as a proposed FCRA bill sparks concern among Church leaders, who fear it could restrict missionary activities. The Opposition alleges the bill targets Churches, impacting BJP's poll prospects in the state.

articles.headline: Parliament Session Likely Extended to Pass Women's Reservation Bill
articles.summary: The budget session of Parliament may be extended for two to three days in April to pass a bill increasing Lok Sabha seats to 816, with 273 reserved for women. The government plans to reconvene the houses after a short gap.

articles.headline: Rajya Sabha Passes CAPF Bill; Opposition Walks Out
articles.summary: The Rajya Sabha passed the Central Armed Police Forces (General Administration) Bill, 2026, by voice vote despite an Opposition walkout. Opponents argue the bill institutionalises deputation, limits CAPF officers' promotion opportunities, and negatively impacts morale, while the government asserts it strengthens internal security.

articles.headline: PM Modi to Hold Mega Roadshow in Thiruvananthapuram on Saturday
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will visit Kerala on April 4 for his second visit in a week, holding a 1.5 km roadshow and public meeting in Thiruvananthapuram. The BJP is intensifying its campaign to win seats in the April 9 Assembly polls, focusing on key constituencies like Nemom.

articles.headline: The ‘Biju Way’: Proactive Security Strategy Legacy
articles.summary: This analysis explores Biju Patnaik's strategic legacy, particularly his role in India's national security during the 1962 Sino-Indian War. Patnaik advocated an aggressive approach, fostering Indo-US cooperation for intelligence (U-2 sorties) and defence, laying groundwork for policies still relevant today, despite Nehru's initial non-alignment.

articles.headline: EC’s New Symbol Rule to Aid Parties in Poll-bound States
articles.summary: The Election Commission of India amended its party symbol rules, lowering the vote share threshold for registered unrecognised political parties (RUPPs) to retain a common symbol. Parties now qualify if they secure 1% of valid votes in either the last two Lok Sabha or Assembly elections, benefiting regional parties like AMMK and ISF.

articles.headline: Mamata Hails SC Order, Warns of NRC and Detention Camps
articles.summary: West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee lauded the Supreme Court's observation on electoral rolls, claiming credit for 22 lakh names included and 18 lakh deleted. She warned citizens of potential NRC implementation and detention camps by the BJP, urging vigilance on polling day against targeted minority vote deletion.

articles.headline: Owaisi Alleges TMC, Others Use Muslims for Votes, Not Development
articles.summary: AIMIM chief Asaduddin Owaisi accused the ruling TMC and other parties of exploiting Muslims for votes in West Bengal without contributing to their development. He urged the community to elect Muslim leaders, asserting that voting for established parties for 50 years had not improved their condition.

articles.headline: PM Modi Chairs Second CCS Meet on West Asia Crisis Impact
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi chaired a second CCS meeting on West Asia to discuss the conflict's impact on agriculture, aviation, shipping, logistics, and MSMEs. Measures were taken to ensure power availability and essential supplies, combat misinformation, and safeguard citizens from economic shocks.

articles.headline: Modi Alleges Congress Reviving Bid to ‘Protect Infiltrators’; Pushes UCC
articles.summary: PM Modi, at Assam rallies, accused Congress of proposing divisive laws to protect infiltrators, a move BJP opposes. He reiterated commitment to Uniform Civil Code (UCC) in Assam, protecting tribal traditions, and highlighted the state's hydrocarbon growth and 5 lakh crore infrastructure investment.

articles.headline: Priyanka Alleges Schemes Used to Compel Women to Attend Rallies in Assam
articles.summary: Congress leader Priyanka Gandhi Vadra alleged that the BJP government in Assam uses women-related schemes to force attendance at party rallies, threatening removal from beneficiary lists. She criticized low tea garden wages, potential post-election fuel price hikes, and Modi's subservience to America.

articles.headline: No Caste, Communal Clashes in TN Unlike BJP-Ruled States: Stalin
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu CM MK Stalin criticized BJP for alleged plots to incite violence, asserting DMK thwarted them. He highlighted peace and effective governance, claiming no caste or communal clashes like in BJP-ruled states. Stalin promised to double financial aid for women to ₹2,000 and provide ₹8,000 coupons for home appliances if DMK returns to power.

articles.headline: Test of Sarma Shiksha Abhiyan: Assam Politics and Development
articles.summary: This analysis delves into Assam's political landscape ahead of elections, focusing on CM Himanta Biswa Sarma's dominance and challenges. It discusses his targeting of Bengali-origin Muslims, BJP's outreach to other communities, visible infrastructure improvements, and lingering issues like floods and the recent tragedy of singer Zubeen Garg.

articles.headline: EC transfers 483 officials in Bengal, 21 times more than other states
articles.summary: The Election Commission of India ordered the transfer of 483 officials in West Bengal since the March 15 poll announcement. This is significantly higher than the 23 transfers in Assam, Kerala, and Tamil Nadu combined. The Trinamool Congress has protested the scale of reshuffling, while the ECI defends the moves based on ground intelligence and 2021 post-poll violence records.

articles.headline: Government and Opposition differ over proposed extension of Parliament budget session
articles.summary: The ongoing budget session may be extended to pass amendments to the Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam for faster implementation of women’s reservation. While the government considers reconvening between April 16 and 18, the Opposition wants any extension to occur after the assembly polls in May to avoid disrupting election campaigning. A two-thirds majority is required for the amendments.

articles.headline: Crude explosive device hurled at Punjab BJP headquarters in Chandigarh
articles.summary: A minor blast occurred outside the Punjab BJP headquarters in Chandigarh on Wednesday evening. No casualties were reported, though a scooter and car sustained minor damage. The pro-Khalistan group Babbar Khalsa International purportedly claimed responsibility for the attack. Police and NIA teams are investigating the site where a crude device was allegedly hurled.

articles.headline: Security forces declare victory against Left Wing Extremism in Bijapur
articles.summary: India has declared the war against Left Wing Extremism (LWE) effectively won in Chhattisgarh’s Bijapur district. For the first time in six decades, no villages remain under Maoist dominance. While security forces continue daily de-mining and patrolling, the local administration is focusing on reopening 264 schools and building 900km of roads to restore normalcy in the region.

articles.headline: PM Modi chairs Cabinet Committee on Security to mitigate conflict impact
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi chaired a CCS meeting to review measures to insulate the Indian economy from the West Asia conflict. Focus areas include diversifying LPG and LNG sources, ensuring stable power supply during summer, and monitoring fertiliser production for the Kharif season. The government is coordinating with state authorities to prevent hoarding and maintain price stability.

articles.headline: CM Siddaramaiah warns voters against supporting independents in Davanagere bypoll
articles.summary: Campaigning for candidate Samarth Shamanur, Chief Minister Siddaramaiah urged voters in Davanagere South to ignore independent candidates. He claimed that such votes would indirectly benefit the BJP and framed the election as a test of his government's welfare record. Siddaramaiah also dismissed allegations of dynastic politics by highlighting families in the BJP.

articles.headline: Priyank Kharge accuses Centre of withholding ₹15,583 crore from Karnataka
articles.summary: Karnataka minister Priyank Kharge alleged that the Union government is "ruthlessly cruel" for denying the state its financial dues, including ₹15,583 crore recommended by the 15th Finance Commission. He also flagged ₹1,943 crore in pending MGNREGA funds. Kharge criticized state BJP leaders for failing to represent the state’s interests regarding these financial shortfalls.

articles.headline: Rajnath Singh attacks LDF government over Sabarimala gold and corruption
articles.summary: Speaking at election rallies in Kerala, Defence Minister Rajnath Singh accused the ruling LDF of betraying Lord Ayyappa. He promised a time-bound CBI probe into alleged gold misappropriation at the Sabarimala shrine. Singh also criticized both the LDF and UDF for appeasement politics and failing to address rising crime, drug use, and unemployment in the state.

articles.headline: Revanth Reddy uses film dialogue to attack Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan
articles.summary: Telangana CM Revanth Reddy targeted Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan during a UDF roadshow by invoking the famous film line "Nee po mone Vijaya." Reddy alleged that the CM's "expiry date has passed" and that his time in power is over. The campaign stop in Nemom was part of efforts to bolster the UDF ahead of the April 9 assembly polls in Kerala.

articles.headline: PM Modi predicts third-term victory for BJP in Assam assembly polls
articles.summary: Addressing a rally in Dhemaji, Prime Minister Narendra Modi expressed confidence that the BJP will secure a "hat-trick" win in Assam. He criticized the Congress for planning "dangerous communal laws" to appease vote banks and accused them of supporting infiltration. Modi highlighted the state's development, specifically mentioning the new semiconductor plant in Jagiroad.

articles.headline: Mamata Banerjee warns TMC candidates of ECI plot to reject nominations
articles.summary: West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee cautioned her party candidates that the Election Commission’s reshuffled officers have been tasked with rejecting TMC nominations. Speaking at a rally in Birbhum, she urged candidates to double-check documents before submission. The BJP responded by stating the TMC is attempting to block legitimate voter enrollment through Form 6.

articles.headline: TMC workers protest at Bengal CEO office over alleged voter roll fraud
articles.summary: Trinamool Congress supporters launched an indefinite protest outside the Chief Electoral Officer’s office in Kolkata. They allege that the BJP is submitting Form 6 applications in bulk to enroll "outsiders" as voters in West Bengal. The protest comes amid reports that seven judicial officers were briefly held up by locals during a voter revision exercise in Malda.

articles.headline: Gujarat local body elections for 10,005 seats scheduled for April 26
articles.summary: The Gujarat State Election Commission announced that polling for 15 municipal corporations, 84 municipalities, and hundreds of panchayats will be held on April 26. Over 27.4 million voters are eligible to participate in the elections, which will utilize EVMs. Polling will take place from 7 am to 6 pm for a total of 10,005 seats across the state.

articles.headline: Three policemen injured in attack on BJP campaign in Tripura
articles.summary: At least three policemen were injured when a BJP campaign event was attacked in West Lembucherra, Tripura. The clash occurred during the run-up to the Tripura Tribal Areas Autonomous District Council elections. BJP leaders condemned the violence and accused TIPRA Motha supporters of vandalizing vehicles and obstructing the democratic process.

articles.headline: Left Front announces seat-sharing deal with ISF for West Bengal polls
articles.summary: The Left Front in West Bengal reached a seat-sharing agreement with the ISF and CPI(ML) Liberation for the 294-member assembly. The CPI(M) will contest 195 seats, while the ISF is allotted 30 and the CPI(ML) Liberation eight. Left Front chairman Biman Bose stated the coalition aims to provide a unified alternative to both the TMC and the BJP.

articles.headline: Priyanka Gandhi accuses Assam government of intimidating female welfare beneficiaries
articles.summary: Senior Congress leader Priyanka Gandhi Vadra alleged that the BJP-led Assam government is forcing women to attend party rallies under threat of losing cash transfer benefits. During a meeting in Nazira, she called the current administration one of "double-slavery" and urged voters to bring Congress back to power to ensure freedom of expression and genuine welfare.

articles.headline: MK Stalin claims DMK government prevented BJP-orchestrated riots in Tamil Nadu
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu CM MK Stalin accused the BJP of conspiring to create caste and religious riots, claiming the DMK successfully thwarted these efforts. Speaking in Tiruchirappalli, Stalin highlighted the state's peaceful environment as a key driver for investment. He also defended the "Illatharasi" welfare scheme against criticism from AIADMK leader Edappadi K Palaniswami.

articles.headline: Palaniswami promises to eradicate drugs in Tamil Nadu within three months
articles.summary: AIADMK general secretary Edappadi K Palaniswami asserted that if his party wins the assembly elections, it will make Tamil Nadu drug-free in three months. He accused the DMK of allowing widespread drug sales with the support of party functionaries. Palaniswami also rebutted "Sanghi" labels from CM Stalin by showing photos of Karunanidhi with former PM Atal Bihari Vajpayee.

articles.headline: CPI(M) leader MA Baby confident of LDF victory in Kerala polls
articles.summary: In an interview, CPI(M) general secretary MA Baby predicted a third consecutive term for the LDF in Kerala. He credited the Pinarayi Vijayan government with eradicating absolute poverty and maintaining communal harmony. Baby also criticized the Congress for its "soft Hindutva" approach and blamed the emergence of Naxalism on the economic policies of both Congress and BJP.

articles.headline: Government withholds FCRA Amendment Bill following Opposition protests in Kerala
articles.summary: The Union government deferred the Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Amendment Bill, 2026, which would have allowed a "designated authority" to seize NGO assets. The move followed significant protests by Opposition leaders and concerns that it would target minority-run organizations in poll-bound Kerala. The government clarified the bill is not shelved but was delayed due to timing.

articles.headline: PM Modi praises Biju Patnaik as BJP MP Nishikant Dubey apologizes
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi hailed former Odisha CM Biju Patnaik as a symbol of courage and nation-building. The praise follows a controversy sparked by BJP MP Nishikant Dubey’s remarks linking Patnaik to the CIA and Nehru during the 1962 war. Dubey issued an unconditional apology, stating his comments were misconstrued, after facing backlash from the BJD and his own party.

articles.headline: Navy was minutes from striking Pakistan in Op Sindoor
articles.summary: Navy Chief Admiral Dinesh K Tripathi revealed the Indian Navy was minutes away from striking Pakistan during Operation Sindoor after the Pulwama attack, stopping kinetic actions only when Pakistan requested. He also noted 1,900 vessels stranded in West Asia due to the ongoing conflict.

articles.headline: India, US continue talks for interim trade deal
articles.summary: India and the US will continue working towards finalizing an interim trade agreement, despite talks slowing after recent developments like the US revoking President Trump's tariff authority. A USTR report highlighted India's high applied tariffs on goods like vegetable oils, automobiles, and medicines, though India reduced some in Budget 2026.

articles.headline: Lok Sabha panel meets MCA on rules, regulations
articles.summary: The Lok Sabha committee on subordinate legislation met with Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA) officials to discuss progress on framing rules and regulations under various Acts, including the Competition Act. The panel examines whether government rule-making aligns with the Constitution and relevant Acts.

articles.headline: 1984 riot victims' lawyer HS Phoolka joins BJP
articles.summary: Senior Supreme Court advocate and former AAP leader H.S. Phoolka, known for representing 1984 anti-Sikh riot victims, joined the BJP. This move strengthens BJP in Punjab ahead of next year's Assembly polls, welcomed by Union Minister Hardeep Singh Puri.

articles.headline: Kerala CM refutes 'debt trap' claims, highlights achievements
articles.summary: Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan dismisses 'debt trap' claims as a distortion of facts, asserting the state's strong fiscal management. He highlighted achievements: first extreme poverty-free state, top rank in Ease of Doing Business, 7,000 startups, and ~1.23 trillion transferred to local bodies.

articles.headline: Trump says US to leave Iran quickly, hints at Nato exit
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump told Reuters the US would be "out of Iran pretty quickly" but could return for "spot hits." He also stated he was considering withdrawing the US from Nato, calling it a "paper tiger," and rejected Iran's ceasefire claim.

articles.headline: US top court skeptical of Trump's birthright citizenship order
articles.summary: US Supreme Court justices signaled skepticism towards President Donald Trump's executive order restricting birthright citizenship. Plaintiffs argued it violates the 14th Amendment. Trump, who attended the arguments, says granting citizenship incentivizes illegal immigration and "birth tourism."

articles.headline: Pre-poll surveys signal tight race between LDF & UDF but NDA may spring a surprise
articles.summary: Pre-poll surveys for Kerala's April 9 Assembly elections indicate a tight race between the CPI(M)-led Left Democratic Front (LDF) and the Congress-led United Democratic Front (UDF). The UDF is projected to win 69-81 seats with a 39% vote share, while the LDF is forecast for 57-69 seats with a 36% vote share. The BJP-led NDA may spring surprises.

articles.headline: 'Cong may bring law to protect infiltrators': Modi
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi, addressing a poll rally in Gogamukh, Assam, alleged that the Congress, if it comes to power, will introduce a law to protect infiltrators. He claimed the Congress aims to turn the majority community into a minority and create a permanent vote bank for infiltrators, accusing the party of dividing the country.

articles.headline: In Bengal, candidates do odd jobs to ‘woo’ voters
articles.summary: In West Bengal's election season, candidates are engaging in unconventional campaigning, such as washing utensils, carrying water buckets for the elderly, or shaving a voter's beard at a roadside saloon. This approach is aimed at connecting with voters on a personal level and mitigating the 'outsider' tag.

articles.headline: BJP, Cong delay candidate lists in TN; early edge for regional parties
articles.summary: With only five days left for nominations for the Tamil Nadu Assembly elections, both national parties, the BJP and Congress, are yet to announce their candidates. This delay reflects internal pressures and strategic dilemmas, giving regional players like DMK, AIADMK, and TVK an early advantage, having already filed nominations.

articles.headline: Vijay’s crowds won’t automatically translate into votes: CPI(M) leader
articles.summary: P Shanmugam, Secretary of the CPI(M) in Tamil Nadu, questioned the narrative around large crowds gathering for actor-turned-politician C Joseph Vijay, stating such turnout shouldn't be misconstrued as electoral strength. He believes Vijay may secure some vote share but has no chance of winning a single seat, despite acknowledging the demographic seeking alternatives.

articles.headline: Kerala BJP candidate’s flex board sparks buzz
articles.summary: A flex board listing former Hindu legislators from the Guruvayur constituency appeared as part of BJP candidate B Gopalakrishnan’s campaign, drawing widespread attention. This followed his controversial ‘Hindu MLA’ remark. Gopalakrishnan stated it was merely a documentation of the constituency’s political past.

articles.headline: Democracy at the doorstep: Home voting begins for super seniors, persons with disabilities in Kerala
articles.summary: Kerala has initiated home voting for 51,993 eligible voters, including 36,017 super seniors (85+) and 15,906 persons with disabilities (PwDs), for the upcoming Assembly elections. Polling teams assist voters with paper ballots, although some complaints about scheduled visits not materializing have been reported.

articles.headline: Will renegade Sudhakaran give CPI(M) a scare in Ambalapuzha?
articles.summary: The Ambalapuzha constituency is witnessing a high-stakes political battle as G Sudhakaran, a seasoned LDF veteran and former PWD Minister, contests as an independent candidate after breaking away from the CPI(M). The UDF has extended its full support, posing a serious challenge to the LDF in its traditional bastion.

articles.headline: ‘New capital will catalyse growth of AP, support Swarnandhra Vision 2047’
articles.summary: Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu stated that the Lok Sabha's unanimous passage of the Bill recognizing Amaravati as Andhra Pradesh's sole capital guarantees its future development. He expressed that the new capital will be a milestone in achieving 'Swarnandhra Pradesh' and thanked Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Union Home Minister Amit Shah for their support.

articles.headline: Ministry of Defence fully utilises capital outlay of ₹1.86 lakh crore for FY26
articles.summary: The Ministry of Defence (MoD) fully utilized its capital outlay of ₹1.86 lakh crore for defence services in FY26, exceeding the original appropriation. A significant portion of this expenditure went towards the acquisition of aircraft, shipbuilding, and modernizing armed forces, demonstrating sustained efforts in defence modernization and infrastructural development in border areas.

articles.headline: Major Policy Announcement Affects National Landscape
articles.summary: The government has unveiled a significant new policy, detailed discussions are underway regarding its implications for various sectors. Experts are analyzing the potential impact on citizens and the national economy, with initial reactions mixed.

articles.headline: Political Roundup: Key State Elections
articles.summary: A roundup of recent political developments, including insights into key state elections and party strategies. Analysts provided perspectives on the evolving political landscape and upcoming legislative sessions.

articles.headline: Public Opinion on New Policies
articles.summary: A survey on public opinion regarding recently introduced government policies revealed diverse perspectives. The feedback will inform future adjustments and communication strategies to ensure public support.

articles.headline: Local Government Reforms Initiated
articles.summary: Local government reforms have been initiated to improve administrative efficiency and public service delivery. The changes aim to empower local bodies and ensure more responsive governance structures.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Debate on Budget
articles.summary: A heated parliamentary debate on the national budget saw opposition parties questioning government spending priorities. The finance minister defended the allocations, citing economic growth targets.

articles.headline: Political Parties Gear Up for Elections
articles.summary: Major political parties are gearing up for upcoming elections, holding rallies and announcing manifestos. Campaign strategies are focusing on key voter concerns and regional issues.

articles.headline: Opposition Leader Criticizes Government
articles.summary: The opposition leader sharply criticized the government's handling of economic issues during a press conference. Calls for greater transparency and accountability were made amid rising public concerns.

articles.headline: Regional Political Alliance Formed
articles.summary: A new regional political alliance has been formed ahead of state elections, aiming to consolidate power and challenge existing parties. Strategies for the upcoming campaign are being finalized.

articles.headline: Government Procurement Policy Reviewed
articles.summary: The government's procurement policy is under review to enhance transparency and efficiency in public spending. The changes aim to ensure fair competition and value for taxpayer money in tenders.

articles.headline: Public Grievance Redressal System
articles.summary: A new public grievance redressal system has been launched to address citizen complaints more efficiently. The digital platform aims to improve transparency and accountability in government services.

articles.headline: Chief Minister Holds Public Meeting
articles.summary: The Chief Minister held a large public meeting, addressing local grievances and promising development projects. The event drew a significant crowd, indicating strong public engagement with political leaders.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Session Debates
articles.summary: The parliamentary session saw intense debates on various national issues, with lawmakers from different parties presenting their views. Key legislation is expected to be passed during this session.

articles.headline: Policy on Digital Privacy Rights
articles.summary: A new policy on digital privacy rights is being formulated to protect citizens' online data. The legislation aims to address concerns regarding data collection, usage, and security in the digital realm.

articles.headline: State Election Results Analyzed
articles.summary: State election results are being analyzed, with implications for national political dynamics. Political analysts offer insights into voter behavior and potential coalition formations.

articles.headline: Political Dialogue on Key Issues
articles.summary: Political dialogue continues on key national issues, including social welfare and economic reforms. Party leaders are engaging in cross-party discussions to build consensus on critical legislative agendas.

articles.headline: Local Election Campaigns Heat Up
articles.summary: Local election campaigns are heating up, with candidates engaging in extensive door-to-door canvassing and public debates. Voter turnout is expected to be high as local issues take center stage.

articles.headline: Government Initiatives for Public Welfare
articles.summary: The government announced new initiatives focused on public welfare, covering housing, food security, and social protection. These programs aim to uplift marginalized communities and reduce inequality across states.

articles.headline: Political Reforms to Enhance Governance
articles.summary: Political reforms are being implemented to enhance governance, transparency, and accountability across all levels of administration. Citizen participation in policy-making processes is also being encouraged.

articles.headline: Government Transparency Initiatives
articles.summary: New government transparency initiatives aim to improve public access to information and reduce corruption. Digital platforms are being implemented to provide real-time updates on public projects and spending.

articles.headline: Political Campaigns for State Elections
articles.summary: Political campaigns for upcoming state elections are intensifying, with parties focusing on local issues and development promises. Rallies and public outreach programs are key components of their strategies.

articles.headline: Karnataka Draft Policy to Tackle Student Phone Addiction
articles.summary: Karnataka unveiled a draft policy addressing excessive and unsafe digital technology use among students. The policy focuses on mental health, cyber safety, and responsible digital behavior through a structured school-based framework, citing links to anxiety, sleeplessness, and social isolation.

articles.headline: MSRamaiah College Hosts Intra-Collegiate Cultural Fest 'XTASY2K26'
articles.summary: MSRamaiah College of Arts, Science and Commerce hosted 'XTASY2K26', an intra-collegiate cultural fest showcasing student creativity. The event featured band performances, a fashion show, singing, dance, and theatre, and was graced by Kannada film personalities like Ragini Dwivedi and Kaviraj.

articles.headline: Non-Engineering Undergrads Bag Top Placement Offers in Tough Year
articles.summary: Despite a challenging year, top non-engineering undergraduate colleges like St Stephen’s and LSR reported strong placements. Meesho offered the highest package (₹36.25 LPA) at both. Loyola College and Christ University also saw higher average salaries and increased job offers, reflecting confidence in their talent pool.

articles.headline: Education Sector Reforms Proposed
articles.summary: Proposals for significant reforms in the education sector were presented, aiming to enhance curriculum and teaching methodologies. The changes are expected to impact students and educators nationwide.

articles.headline: University Admissions Policy Updates
articles.summary: Updates to university admissions policies for the upcoming academic year were released. The changes aim to promote diversity and ensure equitable access to higher education for all students.

articles.headline: Educational Technology Integration
articles.summary: Schools are increasingly integrating educational technology to enhance learning experiences. Training programs for teachers are underway to maximize the benefits of digital tools in classrooms.

articles.headline: Curriculum Updates for Primary Education
articles.summary: Curriculum updates for primary education are being implemented to foster critical thinking and creativity in young learners. Teacher training workshops are a key part of this educational reform.

articles.headline: Adult Literacy Campaigns Launched
articles.summary: Adult literacy campaigns have been launched in several districts, aiming to empower individuals through education. Volunteer networks are supporting the outreach and teaching efforts in communities.

articles.headline: Higher Education Reforms Announced
articles.summary: Significant reforms in higher education were announced, focusing on research excellence and industry partnerships. The goal is to enhance the global competitiveness of universities and graduates.

articles.headline: Digital Education Platforms Launched
articles.summary: New digital education platforms have been launched to provide flexible learning opportunities, especially for remote students. These platforms offer online courses and educational resources.

articles.headline: Skill Development for Digital Economy
articles.summary: Skill development programs are being tailored for the digital economy, focusing on coding, data science, and cybersecurity. These initiatives aim to prepare the workforce for future job markets.

articles.headline: Digital Literacy Programs for Seniors
articles.summary: Digital literacy programs for seniors are being introduced to help them navigate the online world and access digital services. These initiatives aim to bridge the generational technology gap.

articles.headline: Teacher Training and Development
articles.summary: Focus on teacher training and professional development programs to enhance teaching quality across schools. New methodologies and digital tools are being integrated into the curriculum for educators.

articles.headline: Vocational Skills for Employment
articles.summary: Vocational skills training programs are proving effective in enhancing employment prospects for young graduates. Industry partnerships ensure that curriculum aligns with current market demands.

articles.headline: Online Learning Resources Developed
articles.summary: New online learning resources are being developed for students of all ages, offering flexible and accessible educational content. These platforms support both formal and informal learning paths.

articles.headline: University Research Grants Announced
articles.summary: New university research grants were announced to foster innovation and academic excellence. The funding supports projects in science, technology, and humanities, promoting a vibrant research ecosystem.

articles.headline: Digital Skills Training for Youth
articles.summary: Digital skills training programs for youth are preparing them for future job markets. These initiatives cover coding, data analysis, and online content creation, fostering a new generation of tech-savvy workers.

articles.headline: Digital Education Access Expands
articles.summary: Access to digital education is expanding, particularly in remote areas, through government-backed initiatives. These programs provide online learning resources and devices to ensure equitable educational opportunities.

articles.headline: Inclusive Education Policies
articles.summary: New inclusive education policies are being formulated to ensure equitable access and opportunities for students with disabilities. Specialized resources and support systems are key components.

articles.headline: Skill Development Centers Opened
articles.summary: New skill development centers have been opened across the country to provide vocational training. These centers focus on industry-specific skills, aiming to enhance employability and reduce unemployment rates.

articles.headline: Education Access for Marginalized Groups
articles.summary: Programs to improve education access for marginalized groups are yielding positive results. Scholarships, free tuition, and special coaching are helping students pursue higher studies.

articles.headline: Sikkim to close 17 primary schools due to low student enrollment
articles.summary: The Sikkim government has decided to temporarily close 17 primary schools starting from the 2026-27 academic session due to a significant decline in student enrollment. Education officials cited a low Total Fertility Rate (TFR) in the state and a growing preference for private schools as primary reasons. Affected students and teachers will be relocated to the nearest government institutions.

articles.headline: Karnataka govt teachers to begin bilingual teaching in 27,000 schools
articles.summary: The Karnataka Education Department announced that over 27,000 government primary schools will adopt a bilingual teaching model starting next academic year. Teachers will use both Kannada and English to explain concepts, particularly in science and mathematics. The initiative aims to improve English proficiency among students while maintaining a strong foundation in their mother tongue.

articles.headline: Current election cycle critical for Muslim representation in state assemblies
articles.summary: An HT analysis reveals that the four states and one UT going to polls account for 41.6% of all Muslim MLAs in India. West Bengal, Kerala, and Assam are crucial as the probability of electing Muslim MLAs rises significantly in districts where the community’s population share exceeds 25%. Currently, 279 out of 4,123 MLAs nationwide are Muslims, representing a share of 6.8%.

articles.headline: India's digital Census 2027 begins, data needs careful handling
articles.summary: India's decennial Census formally began digitally on April 1 after pandemic delays. The two-phase exercise, including caste enumeration for the first time since 1931, will allow self-enumeration. Politically sensitive issues like delimitation of Lok Sabha seats based on population changes must be skilfully managed to avoid destabilizing social fabric.

articles.headline: Regional Developments Discussed by Officials
articles.summary: Officials met to discuss various regional developments and their impact on local communities. Key points included infrastructure projects and public services improvements, aiming for broader societal benefits.

articles.headline: Social Program Initiatives Launched
articles.summary: Several new social program initiatives were launched, targeting underprivileged communities. The programs aim to provide essential support and improve living standards through various welfare measures.

articles.headline: Food Security Program Assessment
articles.summary: An assessment of the national food security program highlighted its successes and areas for improvement. The review focuses on ensuring adequate food supply and nutritional support for all citizens.

articles.headline: Government Initiatives for Rural Welfare
articles.summary: Government initiatives aimed at improving rural welfare are showing promising results. Programs focus on poverty alleviation, job creation, and access to essential services in underserved communities.

articles.headline: Monkey keeps vigil by Channapatna woman’s body in poignant farewell
articles.summary: In a touching incident in Channapatna, a wild monkey spent the entire night guarding the body of an 85-year-old woman who used to feed it. Local residents reported that the animal remained by the bedside and even touched the woman's face during the funeral procession. The bond between the elderly woman and the monkey had become well-known in the village over several years.

articles.headline: Public Health Emergency Readiness Enhanced
articles.summary: Public health authorities are enhancing emergency preparedness and response with new training programs, drills, and improved resource coordination. This follows recent regional outbreaks, focusing on rapid response mechanisms, community resilience, and readiness for future health crises.

articles.headline: Maternal & Child Health Programs Expand, Show Progress
articles.summary: Maternal and child health programs are expanding, showing significant progress with reduced infant mortality and malnutrition rates. Key initiatives include new vaccination drives, maternal care, and improved access to healthcare and nutrition services.

articles.headline: No GAAR on Pre-2017 Investments, Finance Ministry Clarifies
articles.summary: India's Finance Ministry and CBDT clarified that General Anti-Avoidance Rules (GAAR) won't apply to investments made before April 1, 2017, offering relief to PE/VC firms, foreign investors, and MNCs. This move, following a Supreme Court ruling, aims to restore investor confidence by safeguarding legacy assets, even if gains arise after 2017.

articles.headline: India's March GST Collections Hit Record ₹2 Lakh Crore
articles.summary: India's gross GST collections hit a record ₹2,00,006 crore in March 2026, marking the first time monthly revenue surpassed ₹2 lakh crore, with an 8.8-9% Y-o-Y growth. This was fueled by 18% import revenue and 6-18% domestic transactions, alongside increased economic activity and compliance. Total FY26 collections reached ₹22.27 lakh crore (8.3% growth). Net collections for March were ₹1.77 lakh crore. Experts forecast cautious April growth.

articles.headline: SEZ Units Get One-Year Customs Duty Relief on Local Sales
articles.summary: The government announced a one-year customs duty concession for Special Economic Zone (SEZ) units, valid until March 31, 2027. This relief allows SEZs to sell goods domestically at reduced rates (6.5-15%), helping exporters utilize idle capacity amid global uncertainties. The benefit is restricted to units that commenced production before March 31.

articles.headline: NowPurchase Raises ₹80 Crore Led by Bajaj Finserv
articles.summary: AI-enabled B2B metals procurement platform NowPurchase raised ₹80 crore in a funding round led by Bajaj Finserv, with participation from InfoEdge Ventures, Orios Venture Partners, and individual investors from Real Ispat Group and Lloyds Metals. The capital will scale offerings for the manufacturing sector, highlighting interest in supply chain solutions.

articles.headline: SpaceX Files Confidentially for June IPO, Valued Over $1.75 Trillion
articles.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX has confidentially filed draft IPO papers with the US SEC for a June listing, eyeing a valuation over $1.75 trillion. The listing, potentially raising $75 billion, follows its acquisition of xAI, making it a leader among mega-IPOs, ahead of AI rivals like OpenAI and Anthropic.

articles.headline: Rediff.com India Files Confidential IPO Papers with Sebi
articles.summary: Rediff.com India Ltd, an AvenuesAI subsidiary, filed preliminary IPO papers confidentially with Sebi. This route allows initial feedback without public disclosure, offering flexibility during market volatility and signaling renewed interest in listings by established tech brands. AvenuesAI provides digital payment solutions and enterprise software.

articles.headline: IndiGo Appoints Willie Walsh CEO; Shares Jump
articles.summary: IndiGo appointed industry veteran Willie Walsh, former IATA head, as CEO, causing shares to jump 4.4%. This "swift resolution" after Pieter Elbers' departure signals a focus on international expansion and operational improvements to enhance margins, navigating high costs from West Asia conflict and airspace restrictions.

articles.headline: Capital Market Stocks Rally After RBI Defers Exposure Norms
articles.summary: Capital market stocks, including brokerage and asset managers, surged after RBI deferred new exposure norms until next quarter (July). The rules, requiring 100% cash collateral for broker borrowings, were feared to raise capital costs and reduce leverage. This averted a liquidity squeeze, though the rally's sustainability depends on earnings and global conditions.

articles.headline: India's EV Sales Surge in FY26, e4Ws Double
articles.summary: India's EV market surged in FY26 due to a strong year-end push, driven by expanding charging infrastructure, diverse models, improved supply chains, accessible financing, and competitive pricing. e4W registrations nearly doubled (91.3% Y-o-Y) to 193,633 units, while e2Ws grew 21.8% to 1.40 million units. EV share for FY26 is ~9%.

articles.headline: Adani Ports Hits 500 MT Cargo in FY26, Targets 1 BN by 2030
articles.summary: Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone (APSEZ) handled a landmark 500 million tonnes of cargo in FY26, an 11.1% Y-o-Y increase. The company, operating 19 ports across India and overseas since 1998, reaffirmed its target to handle 1 billion tonnes annually by 2030.

articles.headline: NSE, IGX Partner to Launch Natural Gas Derivatives
articles.summary: NSE and Indian Gas Exchange (IGX) partnered to launch exchange-traded derivatives based on domestic natural gas prices, with NSE introducing futures contracts linked to IGX's benchmark index. This initiative aims to create robust risk management tools, enhance market efficiency, and deepen liquidity across the gas value chain for all stakeholders.

articles.headline: M&M Dethrones Hyundai as No. 2 in FY26 PV Sales
articles.summary: For the first time in a financial year, Mahindra & Mahindra (M&M) displaced Hyundai to become India's second-largest domestic passenger vehicle maker in FY26, selling 6,60,276 units (Hyundai: 5,84,906). Tata Motors also surpassed Hyundai, marking Hyundai's slip to fourth since the late 1990s.

articles.headline: Digital Sectors Continue Strong Growth
articles.summary: Both the E-commerce and IT sectors show strong, continued growth. E-commerce is driven by internet penetration and online shopping, reshaping retail and logistics. The IT sector expands via digital transformation, outsourcing, and investments in AI and cloud services.

articles.headline: SIM-Binding Deadline Extended for Messaging Apps to Dec 2026
articles.summary: The government extended the SIM-binding deadline for messaging platforms like WhatsApp and Telegram to December 31, 2026, requiring apps to work only with an active SIM. The mandatory six-hour web logout rule was removed, replaced by risk-analysis-based logouts, addressing user-experience concerns and easing compliance pressure on platforms.

articles.headline: OpenAI Valued at $852 Billion After $122 Billion Funding
articles.summary: OpenAI completed its largest funding round to date, raising $122 billion at an $852 billion valuation. Amazon invested $50 billion (contingent on IPO), with Nvidia and SoftBank each contributing $30 billion. This financing bolsters OpenAI's costly push for more chips, data centers, and talent for AI acceleration.

articles.headline: Starlink Partners Meghalaya for Satellite Internet in Remote Areas
articles.summary: Elon Musk's Starlink and the Meghalaya government signed an agreement to provide high-speed satellite internet in remote and hilly areas. This major state-level collaboration aims to bridge the digital divide and improve access to education and healthcare in underserved regions by leveraging Starlink's technology.

articles.headline: UPI Transactions Hit Record ₹29.53 Lakh Crore in March
articles.summary: Unified Payments Interface (UPI) transactions hit a record high in March, reaching ~₹29.53 lakh crore in value and 22.64 billion in volume. Fuelled by festivities and fiscal year closure, this marked ~19% value and ~24% volume Y-o-Y growth. FY26 saw a total of ~₹314.23 trillion in value and ~241.616 billion in volume.

articles.headline: Perplexity AI Sued for Allegedly Sharing User Data
articles.summary: Perplexity AI faces a class-action lawsuit in San Francisco for allegedly sharing users' personal information with Meta Platforms and Google without consent. The suit claims trackers give these companies access to AI search engine conversations for targeted advertising and data resale, breaching California computer privacy and fraud laws.

articles.headline: 5G Technology Rollout Accelerates, Expanding Nationwide
articles.summary: The deployment of 5G technology is accelerating and expanding to more cities nationwide. This promises faster internet speeds and improved connectivity, expected to revolutionize industries, enhance consumer experiences, and boost the digital economy and innovation.

articles.headline: Quantum Computing Research Shows Promising Progress
articles.summary: Significant progress and advancements in quantum computing research are ongoing, with scientists developing stable quantum bits and overcoming technical challenges. This revolutionary technology holds promising potential to revolutionize computation, data security, and practical applications.

articles.headline: NHAI Exceeds FY26 Highway Construction Target by 15%
articles.summary: The National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) exceeded its FY26 construction target by 15%, building 5,313 km of highways against a 4,640 km goal. Capital expenditure reached over ₹2.44 lakh crore, surpassing budgetary support by 2.5%, demonstrating robust infrastructure development despite a moderation from the previous year.

articles.headline: Minister Patil Requests Bengaluru-Vijayapura Express Train
articles.summary: Karnataka Industries Minister MB Patil requested Union Railway Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw to launch a Bengaluru-Vijayapura express train. The request follows the March 25 completion of the Alamatti–Wandal double-line work. The service aims to cut travel time from 15 to 10 hours, benefiting students and business travelers from Bagalkote and Vijayapura districts.

articles.headline: Smart City Projects Achieve Key Milestones
articles.summary: Smart city projects show significant progress, achieving key milestones in urban development and technological integration. These initiatives, including civic infrastructure and digital integration, aim to improve residents' quality of life, civic amenities, urban living, and public services.

articles.headline: US Supreme Court Skeptical of Trump's Birthright Citizenship Curb
articles.summary: The US Supreme Court expressed skepticism over President Trump's executive order to restrict birthright citizenship for children of non-citizens, signaling likely rejection of his immigration agenda. Justices questioned the constitutionality of upending the 14th Amendment's long-standing interpretation. Trump attended the hearing, a first for a sitting president.

articles.headline: Lok Sabha Passes Jan Vishwas Bill, Decriminalizing Offences
articles.summary: The Lok Sabha passed the Jan Vishwas (Amendment of Provisions) Bill, 2026, amending 784 provisions across 79 Central Acts. It decriminalizes 717 provisions and rationalizes over 1,000 offenses to promote ease of doing business and living, building on the 2023 Act and removing outdated laws.

articles.headline: Legal Aid Services Expand for Vulnerable Populations
articles.summary: Legal aid services are expanding to provide free legal assistance and greater access to justice for marginalized and disadvantaged groups. New centers and public awareness programs are underway to ensure access to justice and protect the rights of vulnerable populations.

articles.headline: IPR Protection & Enforcement Efforts Underway
articles.summary: Efforts are underway to strengthen intellectual property rights (IPR) protection and enforcement. New mechanisms are being considered, and the government is cracking down on piracy and counterfeiting across industries to foster innovation and protect creators.

articles.headline: Trump: US to Exit Iran 'Quickly', Criticizes NATO
articles.summary: President Donald Trump stated the US will exit Iran "pretty quickly," but may return for "spot hits." He expressed disgust with NATO's lack of support in Iran, considering withdrawing the US from the alliance, and warned Iran to "Vacate Hormuz else we'll send you to Stone Age."

articles.headline: International Aid Mobilized for Disaster Relief & Recovery
articles.summary: International aid is being mobilized and coordinated for disaster relief and long-term recovery in a neighboring country affected by natural calamities. Humanitarian organizations are focusing on assistance, support, rebuilding, rehabilitation, and ensuring resilient infrastructure and sustainable livelihoods.

articles.headline: Italy Misses World Cup for Third Consecutive Time
articles.summary: Four-time world champions Italy missed FIFA World Cup qualification for the third consecutive time after a playoff defeat to Bosnia and Herzegovina. The loss, following either a 4-1 penalty shootout or a 1-0 final score with a late Edin Dzeko penalty, marks a historic low for Italian football, leading to calls for structural reforms.

articles.headline: Rizvi, Stubbs Lead Delhi Capitals to IPL Win
articles.summary: Delhi Capitals secured a convincing six-wicket win against Lucknow Super Giants in an IPL match at Ekana Stadium. Sameer Rizvi (unbeaten 70) and Tristan Stubbs (39) guided DC to chase down 173. The win keeps Delhi in the playoff race, while Lucknow's bowling struggled, marking their third successive defeat against the Capitals.

articles.headline: Raja Ravi Varma Painting Sells for Record ₹167.2 Crore
articles.summary: Raja Ravi Varma's 1890s oil painting 'Yashoda and Krishna' sold for a record ₹167.2 crore ($18 million) at a Saffronart auction, becoming the costliest Indian painting ever. This national treasure, previously owned by the Travancore royal family, exceeded its ₹80 crore estimate, surpassing M.F. Husain's record and celebrated for its unique fusion of Indian and European art styles.

articles.headline: New Cultural Exchange Programs Launched for Youth
articles.summary: New cultural exchange programs, including youth initiatives, have launched to foster international and cross-cultural understanding and artistic collaboration. These programs aim to showcase national heritage globally, providing opportunities for young people to experience diverse traditions and promote global citizenship.

articles.headline: FCRA Bill Deferred Amid Opposition Protests, Kerala Polls
articles.summary: The government deferred the contentious Foreign Contribution (Amendment) Regulation Bill, 2026, in Lok Sabha amidst strong Opposition protests and with an eye on Kerala assembly elections, where the Church holds significant influence. Opposition alleged the bill targeted Churches, but the government clarified it was for national security, not against any religion.

articles.headline: Lok Sabha Declares Amaravati Sole Capital of Andhra Pradesh
articles.summary: The Lok Sabha passed the Andhra Pradesh Reorganisation (Amendment) Bill, 2026, officially declaring Amaravati as the state's sole capital, effective June 2, 2024. This move, which settles years of contention over a three-capital plan and was supported by ruling BJP and major opposition parties despite YSRCP protest, fulfills a long-pending demand for a focused administrative hub.

articles.headline: Actor Vijay Files Nomination for Tiruchirappalli East
articles.summary: Actor-turned-politician and TVK chief Vijay is set to file his nomination papers for the Tiruchirappalli (East) assembly constituency on April 2. He is contesting from two seats, the other being Perambur in Chennai, and will submit papers at the Trichy Corporation office for the upcoming April 23 Tamil Nadu Assembly elections.

articles.headline: Lok Sabha Declares Amaravati Sole Capital of Andhra Pradesh
articles.summary: The Lok Sabha passed the Andhra Pradesh Reorganisation (Amendment) Bill, 2026, declaring Amaravati as the state's sole and permanent capital, effective June 2, 2024. Hailed by CM N. Chandrababu Naidu, this bill, passed via voice vote despite YSRCP protest, settles years of contention, removes uncertainty, fulfills reorganization spirit, and prevents future changes to its status.

articles.headline: Government Reforms Ongoing in Public Sector
articles.summary: Government reforms in the public sector are ongoing, aiming to improve governance, accountability, and enhance efficiency. Initiatives include digitizing services, streamlining bureaucratic processes, and reducing hurdles for greater transparency and improved service delivery.

articles.headline: CBSE Launches AI & CT Curriculum for Classes 3-8
articles.summary: CBSE, with the Ministry of Education, launched a new AI and Computational Thinking curriculum for classes 3-8, starting 2026-27. Developed by IIT Madras experts, it covers digital footprints, privacy, and logical thinking. Implemented across 32,900 schools, it aims to build AI-readiness and introduce foundational CT skills, aligned with NEP 2020.

articles.headline: New Vocational Training Programs Launched for Youth
articles.summary: New vocational training programs have launched, targeting unemployed youth to equip them with employable skills. These initiatives aim to bridge skill gaps in various industries, including emerging sectors like digital marketing and renewable energy, thereby boosting youth employment rates.

articles.headline: India's First Digital Census 2027 Begins with Self-Enumeration
articles.summary: India launched its first digital Census 2027, the world's largest enumeration, with Phase I (Houselisting and Housing Census) now active. President Murmu, PM Modi, and HM Shah self-enumerated. Available in 16 languages via a web portal, it commenced in eight states/UTs (including Delhi/Karnataka), seeing ~55,000 households use the digital self-enumeration facility on day one.
